text:
#2437
Being an introverted girl and studying engineering—what a struggle that is. I’m sure many can relate. And then there’s KUET—so male-dominated. The minority of girls often get overshadowed by the overwhelming number of boys. Maybe I’m wrong. Maybe it just feels this way because I’m in first year. Maybe things change in second, third, or fourth year. I truly hope they do. I hope we are treated equally and given the same importance.

Besides, there are already so many limitations for girls. A boundary we can’t quite cross. Not everyone, of course. But people like me—introverts. We don’t even have communication skills, let alone any idea of how to improve them. We don’t know how we’re going to survive these four years. And then what about professional life? We don’t even have any sort of community forming around us. So how will we get help? We’re clueless—completely clueless. It just feels like we’re drifting, floating somewhere unknown. Will we ever reach the shore? Or are we going to drown?

~A frustrated introvert girl from 1st year (24 batch)

text:
#2435
If you’re feeling down, please know, you are not alone.
Talk to someone close, someone who cares. And even if you feel like there’s no one, just pause and think about your parents, the ones who love you beyond words. Imagine their pain if you ever chose to give up.

Your life is not just your own, it’s a precious gift.
You don’t have the right to destroy it out of despair. Life is a journey of ups and downs, yes. But every storm passes, and you must keep fighting, with hope in your heart, till your very last breath.

And the truest peace, the strongest strength, comes from faith.
Turn to Allah.
Pray. Cry to Him. Trust in His plan. Religious duties are not just obligations, they’re anchors that hold us steady in rough seas.

Yes, sometimes we break down. It’s part of being human. But after every breakdown, take a deep breath, gather your courage, and keep going.

Because I care. Deeply.
And I want you to know, you matter. Your existence has purpose. You are loved more than you realize.

~vinsmoke

You are loved more than you realize.

text:
Hey, 
I don't know what kind of pain or struggles you had to go through to feel this way, and I'm not going even try to pretend to fully understand it. But, what I do know is you and your life is worth a lot more than the hurt/hollowness you're feeling right now. It might seem impossible, like there's no way out, but that's only your pain telling you. You are definitely stronger this 

Suicide is a permanent action (no, it's not a solution) to what is often a temporary situation. Please, take a moment to think about the aftershocks your absence would leave, not only in the lives of your close ones, but in all the things you might miss (healing, growth, and the future). And also, the world would miss a whole lot of you:) 

So, please, don't take any sudden action. Talk with people, reach out to someone (Your family, friend, batchmate, seniors, your favourite tonger mama or just anyone) . Even just holding on for a day matters a lot:) And if (and only if) you feel unable to open to people, you can just write whatever you have on your mind in the form, and you'll see the magic in the comments.

YOU MATTER. YOU'RE NOT ALONE. AND IT'S OKAY TO ASK FOR HELP.

text:
#2430
Spoiler alert (well kinda ig)

Never thought "The Big Bang Theory" theme song could make me emotional, but it actually did when they started playing that acoustic version at the end, with the seven of them sitting together, chatting, laughing, eating Chinese food one last time. The void I feel every time I finish watching a sitcom is so real. It feels like a big part of my life just came to an end – well, at least until I start watching another one lol. It’s both funny and amazing how deeply attached you can get to a bunch of fictional characters, to the point where they feel like a second family, a comfort zone, an escape from reality. They make you laugh your a$$ off even on your worst days, on days when you forget how to laugh. Honestly, sitcoms are lifesavers.

"Math, science, history, unraveling the mysteries,
That all started with the big bang!" <3

~anonymous

text:
#2419
dear admins, it is for you, watashi no tomodachi , actually aro age eta ekhane likhte chaisilam but likhbo likhbo kore likha hoyni. Ig it's time. I might not show it on my face but you people are actually quite dear to me. eta emon na je goto 4 mash bondhe realize korsi but I had this feeling since day 1 of meeting you people, je perhaps these are the new characters of the new season of my life, mukhe jotoi boli na keno je ew tora shob filler character. Thing is KUET e eshe I came across scenarios jegulo I never thought ei jibon e face korte hobe. I actually tried to be independent for once in my life, inside I was battling to be a strong person, but on the outside, I was a complete mess. I gave a very clumsy messy impression in front of you peeps which I perhaps have to bear for life. Then I happen to do such ignorant stuffs which happen to be the doom for my very own classmates(I will perhaps never be able to forgive myself for my ignorance, yet I am so sorry for whatever I did). I also caused complications in other people's friendships and relationships very much unknowingly. But my apologies don't come with pity or looking down upon you people, nor they are empty. My friends at kuet, sometimes they are sad and depressed. I can't do anything mostly except saying "be patient" or "you can do it dw". kichhu kichhu classmate ke toh dekhleo depressed lage na. Pore jante pari je tar backstory Shakespeare er tragedy keo haar manabe. Some are sad and silently battling, especially the guys, who are always assumed to be strong and level headed. Although I am staying anonymous, but all I want to say is (this is what i say to motivate myself)- "life e onek kichhu niyei overthink korsi, onek kichhui bhabsilam parbo na, yet life happened and I lived. Maybe there is something Divine that protects me."

~wwwcat

text:
#2416
Sometimes, I feel like having someone by my side would make it easier to endure the many days that still lie ahead in this campus life. But then again, I wonder, is it really necessary? I'm doing just fine, aren’t I?
There are moments when, just by looking around and seeing others with someone they can lean on, I suddenly feel an emptiness I didn’t even know was there. Somewhere deep inside my heart, a quiet longing rises, maybe I, too, deserve someone like that.
Someone close. Someone I can truly call mine.
In this city of silent depressions, it would be comforting to have just one person who feels like home.
Sometimes, the loneliness is overwhelming.
It’s like, I have everyone, yet I have no one.
And honestly, I don’t even know what to feel anymore...

~vinsmoke

When I first began to taste the bitterness of this world, it hurt me deeply. After every big disappointment, I would tell myself that I’d never go through it again, that I wouldn’t care about anyone or anything. But in reality, I always did. I pretended not to care but deep down, I always did.
Now as time has passed, I’ve reached a point where I genuinely feel nothing. Everything feels numb. Even if I try to care now, I can’t —because somewhere inside, I know I’ll only end up hurting myself again.

~anonymous

text:
#2406
The tragedy that defines us

For so many times I’ve been on the other side of saying goodbyes. Going to the last spot possible and then standing still with my heart in my hand, looking at her. It’s always me who stands and always her never looking back if I’m still there or not. And after you experience the same thing many times, like watching the sun rising from the east and melting away in the west, you tend to accept it as something natural. And like this, my misfortune became trivial, naturally. 

I have been subject to tragedies after tragedies. Everyone does. If you look carefully, you’d see moments of true happiness are countable by hand. Though the slow but sure eruption of happiness, laughter, and kisses fuels our endurance for tragedy, we, nonetheless, are always sad on different levels. In the sac of tragedies, there is one that stinks and never lets us forget that it exists. It lingers and lingers.

Such tragedy defines us against our will. And without it, you’d feel like an incomplete person. Tragedy is not just the feeling of pure pain of the aftermath of something universally bad and horrific. Tragedy is fact, record of the misfortune. It’s history, our history that’s unerasable and ,as I said, defines us.

The tragedy that defined me is the love that I could never retain. She just said you love me too deeply and I can never do justice to your passion. She knows, whatever she does, she’ll end up hurting me. And she did, however passively, by keeping distance. I believe she didn’t want her conscience to weigh on her. By staying with me and giving me a chance to love her more than she could love me, it would have always reminded her that she is doing injustice and she would have to come face to face with my agony.  I guess that’s for the better. But she couldn’t guess that it was impossible for me to move on and that she would keep hurting me in perpetuity.

You see, my great tragedy is not that I lost her. It’s that I have found her. The love, companionship and bonding in her is so perfect that it’s impossible for me to settle for anything else. It’s not perfect in an objective sense. My definition of perfect was defined by her attributes so anything different from her is imperfect and would remind me of her. It’s really Glimpse of Us by Joji all over. And so it becomes misfortune to tragedy to identity. How fragile we can be sometimes. 

There is a version of Glimpse of Us in piano that imitates Chopin. It’s the music that do justice to my pain. 

~Someone from ECE.

Class was on. Projector glowing. Silence everywhere.
Me? Fighting sleep like it’s the final boss.

So I let out a tiny, innocent yawn.

Next thing I know, my friend leans over and whispers:
“Congrats. Your yawn just echoed through the mic. Sir stopped mid-slide and is staring.”

My soul left my body.
But I calmly closed my eyes and went:
“Sir… I was praying for better concentration.” 

Sir said nothing.
But the next slide said:
“Operating System: Sleep Mode.”
I swear that man personally attacked me via PowerPoint.

~anonymous

Well, the thing is, it's not actually "snake". You know, when people you always felt like you included in your life don’t include you — it feels like they betrayed you, and hence the "snake" part comes in. But, but, but — not always is the snake that simple. You know when you see a bunch of girls who were good jigri-jan type friends — yeah my boy, we’re on the same page — we know one day the bond will break. And eventually, we see the group break, and one or two of them start living life separately. Now, you’ll hear two sides of the story. And the greatest decision is in your hands — whether you choose to call the girl who separated a snake, or the bunch a snake.
But my boy, we all know — "তালি কখনও এক হাতে বাজে না।"

~anonymous

text:
#2363
I don’t know… but this pause feels like maybe, just maybe, life’s giving me a way out. A chance to leave that place that’s always made me feel small, worthless… like I never truly belonged. A place where I’ve done nothing but doubt myself, question everything… wondering if I’m even on the right path or just completely lost.

That place has taken away my ability to even picture a future. I’ve never felt this lost before. As a kid, I never thought I’d end up at a place like that… never imagined my life would feel like this—so full of confusion and drowning in self-doubt.

Maybe walking away and starting something I actually care about is what I need. I thought I’d eventually move on… but this unannounced depressing vacation has only made me question everything all over again.

I’m just… tired. Tired of feeling worthless. Tired of the confusion. Tired of the constant self-doubt eating me alive. Tired of pretending I’m okay when I never really felt like I belonged in the first place.

~Someone from *** '23

Someone’s crying with an AirPod in,

It’s been almost four months now, and our university is still closed.

Every week we hear something like, “Maybe next week,” “There’s a meeting coming,” “An announcement is expected soon.” But the reality is—we’re still stuck in the same place.

We’re students. We need regular classes, academic flow, mental preparation for exams—without these, it’s hard to stay focused. Some may be utilizing their time well, but not everyone is in the same mental state.

Just one honest question—how much longer?

Does anyone actually know what’s going on? Or are we all just living on hopes and assumptions?

~Someone from CSE'23

text:
#2331
"Truly, one-sided love is terrifying.
I can only love him from a distance.
When I long to see him,
I merely steal glances—
For I have no right to look directly into his eyes.

I can only pray for his well-being,
Never quite able to ask, “How are you?”
Except during sleep, he’s always on my mind.

I am not a brave woman,
Not brave enough to stand before him and confess my love.
I own him so deeply in my heart
That even hearing someone else speak his name
Tears me apart inside.

In the end, he will belong to the one
Who is destined to have him.
And I?
I remain the silent observer—always."

~anonymous

I have a really big problem—I tend to notice even the tiniest things. Just recently, I was looking at the reactions someone gave on my posts and someone else’s. At one point, that person used to be close to me, so maybe that’s why I checked. I saw that on every post of hers, he had reacted with a “care,” while on mine, sometimes it was a “love,” and sometimes… nothing at all.

It felt like he was trying to silently tell me something through those reactions—that there's a difference between her and me. It felt like he was showing me that *she* is more important in his life… not me.

~anonymous

text:
#2314
If you ever find yourself smiling alone after seeing someone’s message, feeling really good, blushing, and getting the urge to write the first letter of their name with a 25-taka packet of Kaveri mehndi...

If you feel like listening to romantic songs while looking at their picture, and you’re more excited for their birthday than their own mother...

If you’ve already started distancing yourself from your cousins/friends just because they asked you to, and suddenly everything from clothes, notebooks, wallpapers, purses, to shoes in their favorite color starts appealing to you — even to the point that you want to throw away all your parents' expectations just to study what they like...

Then it’s time to take three very important steps:

1. Go to that person’s profile and click on the three dots (•••) on the top left corner.

2. After clicking, you’ll see a symbol of a head and shoulders with the word Block written beside it. Click there.

3. After blocking, recite Ayatul Kursi three times, purse your lips like Donald Duck, and blow over both your shoulders. Never unblock again.

That person you just blocked is a limited edition “Iblis Light”.
They show up in your life just to mess up your perfectly organized peace.
They won’t marry you, won’t commit, they’ll just float around wasting your time, only to disappear later. Say NO to them.

You already have enough chaos in life — don’t ruin yourself listening to sweet nothings at 2 or 3 in the morning.

If you feel too lonely, adopt two cats.
After getting scratched a few times, your craving for romantic scratches will be gone.
Still feel like falling in love? Go ahead — then you'll learn what "how many grains in how many sacks of rice" really means.
Life will turn into something truly nice and attractive. 

Thank me later

~anonymous

#2310
Why are you Important

You walked into my life like a soft breeze—unexpected, gentle, and suddenly everything felt lighter. I don’t remember the exact moment I fell for you… maybe it was when you smiled, or when you said my name like it mattered. All I know is, since you came, nothing feels the same—in the best possible way.

You're important because your eyes have this magic—when I look into them, the world slows down, and it’s just us.
You're important because your touch, even the smallest one, feels like home.
You're important because your presence gives my ordinary days a meaning I never knew I was missing.

I’ve fallen for your love, your smile, and even your anger. And honestly? I’d rather face your storms than live without your sunshine

I love the way you make everything romantic without even trying—how your sleepy voice makes mornings beautiful, how your laugh echoes in my heart long after the sound fades. 

You're not just important to me—you are my reason.
My comfort.
My chaos and calm.
My poetry and peace.
My forever, even when you think you're just being silly or simple.

You’re important because loving you feels like breathing—I don’t think about it, I just do.
And even in a world full of people, it’s always you.It’s you. It will always be you. Its always uss. Uuuuu minnneee.

~your's Typo mistake partner

text:
#2302
As a KUET’24 student, I always thought varsity life would be exciting. You know—new chapter, new friends, big dreams, 4 years of legendary memories.
Reality check: I’m just at home… sleeping like it’s my full-time job.

KUET situation right now? Absolute chaos. Nobody knows when orientation is. Even seniors, especially 19 batch, are still stuck waiting for verdicts. We freshers don’t even have approximate info—nothing. Zero. Niruddesh varsity life.

Meanwhile, my other friends in different unis?
They’re already attending classes, making squad goals, crushing assignments, and flexing campus photos.
And me? I’m just sitting at home and sleeping all day long.

Days? I sleep.
Nights? I overthink life like I’m writing a sad novel.
Where am I going? What’s gonna happen? 

I want to start something skillful. I want to be productive. Do something creative. But with this “will-I-stay-or-not” suspense? Feels like I’m stuck in some Netflix thriller without the skip button.

Old friends have moved on to their new lives. I’m here… counting days, talking to my ceiling, becoming besties with my bed. Even my family looks at me like: “Tor varsity koi?” 

So, dear seniors and KUET warriors —
How do I stop wasting my life sleeping and start doing something useful? Drop anything!

[KUET_Sphinx_M24]

~anonymous

waited in the quiet shade,
While you bloomed beneath her sun.
Hoped you’d see the light I gave
But your heart was already won.

A lesson ,time forgot to teach.

Not out of hate, but out of grace.
You chose her and that’s alright.
I just won’t run a losing race.

text:
#2289
Those of us who are ugly or less handsome than average guys, don’t have huge generational wealth, and are studying in a department in KUET from where you will not be getting a decent salary job in BD.
what will you do after getting out of here? 
You have another war ahead. 
You have to again fight for a job, you have to fight for the girl you want in your life. There’s a 99% chance that you are going to lose her. 
Then you will be left with a lot of disappointments. 
After getting a job, some years later, you will clearly know that hard work can only beat talent, but it cannot beat generational wealth. You have to earn extra to serve your family. 
The guys with privileges may also be facing other problems. They may be facing huge expectations vs. reality checks. They may be facing the huge burden of expectations from their family. Everyone has their own fight.

Years will go on, Suddenly one day, you are going to see your face in the mirror, you will see you are aging, you look like an uncle, you are unmarried. 
Now you have to marry someone, but girls you choose will not accept you again, you will be rejected multiple times because you didn’t have the time to take care of your looks. Again, you have to go through disappointments. 
But somehow you will get married and have kids.

Later, after 10 years, on an early morning in your own home, you will be watching your family your wife, your kids getting ready for school and you are helping your wife in the kitchen before going to the office, all of a sudden, you will feel:
life is beautiful.

~blunt person

Years will go on, Suddenly one day, you are going to see your face in the mirror, you will see you are aging, you look like an uncle, you are unmarried.  Now you have to marry someone, but girls you choose will not accept you again, you will be rejected multiple times because you didn’t have the time to take care of your looks. Again, you have to go through disappointments.  But somehow you will get married and have kids.  Later, after 10 years, on an early morning in your own home, you will be watching your family your wife, your kids getting ready for school and you are helping your wife in the kitchen before going to the office, all of a sudden, you will feel: life is beautiful.

text:
#2264
They say we are shaped by what surrounds us, a truth that's long accepted and rarely questioned.
But whose fault is it when everything around me is just another broken soul, twisted by the dull weight of society?
Why is it that my beliefs, which stand firm in solitude, crumble so easily in their presence?
No matter how hard I try, I can't seem to protect them when I'm among others.
I try not to let it affect me as much anymore. Still, I hate the feeling 
like a pawn on their board,
pushed by unseen hands, never my own

~Riven

ab hum wo ghaltiyaan dubara nhi dohra sakte, warna log kia kahenge?

ab hum bache nhi rahe,

lekin humara bachpana aj bhi hum mai rehta hai.

Like sunshine at the end of the day.

But I'm a whisper in the crowd

No deep hurt, no difficult goodbye.
Only vacant silence in the air
Is this an original or a reference?
A thousand smiles, but none are connected.

I laugh, I speak, I play my part,
Something, however, lingers incomplete in my heart.
Not love, maybe, nor friendship's visage—
Just longing for a sunnier place.

Where one can witness the whole of me
Not just a body—a soul Where words are spoken, and silence is shared And it means I'm loved.

~Someone from 22

text:
#2241
If you're planning to marry a guy, you shouldn’t let just your feelings guide you. Your boyfriend—or future husband—has to be a genuinely good person, not just to you and your closed ones, but to everyone around him. You can’t expect to be happy in a relationship if you’re only leading with love and romance. For a healthy relationship or marriage, you should truly admire the person you’re with.

He should be family-oriented, respectful to everyone, futuristic, protective, provider, ambitious, curious, kind, helpful, hard working, religious—the list goes on. Of course, no one is perfect to only have all the good qualities, but hey you get what im saying! you should have so much trust in your man that you’d confidently believe he’d help elderly strangers on the street, and never mistreat anyone etc etc.

And keep in mind, if you want valuable qualities in your future partner, you should also provide some valuable qualities to actually deserve that person.

I honestly don’t understand why some girls date guys who aren't even decent human beings—like the kind of guy who would make even random girls feel unsafe just walking on the same street. Is it really just because he's romantic and because you feel a spark when you're together? How do you justify marrying someone like that?

I’m saying this from a woman’s perspective, but I’m sure men also look for certain qualities in a wife—at least if they're wise enough not to let their hormones make the decision for them.

~anonymous

text:
#2227
I'm here to share a very simple story. Watching my family gradually slip from middle class to lower-middle class, and eventually to poverty, is not something easy to digest. Seeing a once joyful family slowly fall apart under the weight of hardship is one of the cruellest sights in this world. From going out together for trips, now we are haunted by the constant worry of having three meals a day. Where does this end? What’s the point of living like this? Perhaps that's why news headlines like "Entire family commits suicide by consuming poison" have become so common these days.
They say, "I want to live."

text:
#2207
It was my birthday. I was going out with my friends. On the way, you called me and asked me to wait five minutes before heading out, you had a surprise for me. But I was already out. I stopped and said, “Show it to me nonetheless.”

And then you video called.

There you were, wearing that red and white saree, your hair loose, swaying slightly as you tilted your head and smiled at me through the screen. My heart paused for a beat. That smile, it wasn’t just a smile, it was a story I’d been waiting to read for a long time.

You nodded and said, “Happy birthday. I couldn’t think of a better way to show you how much you mean to me.”

In that moment, the crowd around me disappeared. The city noise, the birthday plans, they all faded away. It was just you. Just us. Words couldn’t form in my mouth.

You laughed-smiled, that soft, familiar sound that always made things feel okay, no matter how hard life got.

“Come back soon” you said. And I swear, if I could’ve flown back right then, I would’ve.

I came home around 9 PM, tired but still glowing from everything. I called you. When your camera turned on…..you were still wearing that saree.

I just froze. “You waited?”

You gave that small nod, your eyes soft. “I told you I wanted to be part of your birthday,” you said. “So I waited for you.”

You stayed dressed like that for me. Just so I could feel like you were with me, even from so far away. Even through the distance. Even through everything.

And in that moment, something inside me cracked open.

Because God, I miss you. Not just your presence, not just your voice, but the way it felt to be near you. To look over and know you were really there. The world felt a little more hollow without you in it. And yet, somehow, you still manage to fill it up with love like this.

But now we’re no longer us. Somewhere along the line, we lost each other. Maybe it was the silence. Maybe it was the weight of things we never said. Or maybe you just stopped choosing me. Whatever it was, we broke and no matter how many times I try to glue those memories back together, they cut me all the same.

I looked at you on the screen that day, still radiant, still etched into the parts of me I can’t unlove. And I realized, no matter the miles, no matter the silence, no matter the goodbye, A part of me is still standing in that moment with you.

We may not be together anymore. But I miss us, the way we used to be, like hell.

I love you more than words can stretch to hold and I miss you like hell and I wish things would've turned out the way we wanted it to be.

~Not so anonymous

text:
#2182
Toxic family, toxic KUET – because I was born unlucky? Not like I have enough money for a safe future, don't have the cg. Don't have so called skill. An uncertain future... 
Suppose you are the youngest in the family. You can call your family middle class. It's not like your parents don't love you, but they don't love, more importantly, respect each other. It's not like they fight every day. Some days, they could even be like any normal husband and wife, discussing something about the house. But in a way, they are always complaining about each other to their children. It seems like they are in a competition to prove the other has more faults. Sometimes they are fighting over the smallest thing, and those fights aren't normal. You have seen your mother trying to commit suicide because of anger. Your whole life, you have seen them, for some reason, never able to accept each other. They always want that their children will hate the other- Ammo wants the children will hate abbo, vice versa
They had an arranged marriage. Their family backgrounds are so different. Their thinking about life, their ways of life, are so different.
The disrespect is so much that sometimes you wonder how they are still together. And the answer is simple – it wasn’t  obvious to divorce in the past. For them, divorce is a very heavy word and something almost impossible to accept by their family or themselves.
You don't even have many friends. Somehow, you are insecure around people. You are scared of marriage- ehat if you get the same type of partner? What if you become like them?  You don't have anyone to share your life with. Sometimes you observe your family and just want to run away... Your parents love you, but they don't support you. You don't care much about your future. More like – as you are the youngest, it doesn't matter much.
They are tired, you are tired.
I am the "you."
Hey KUET, I am tired. I have a toxic family. I don't have many friends, but KUET is my "hide from reality" for these years. After graduation, I will try to move away from this family, as my other siblings did.
Please KUET, enough now. Stop being toxic – not you too, please... I would have no other way but to cry and be in this depression. I can't anymore. Please start the classes
Many will come and suggest a lot of things. Trust me, the fight to live through a toxic family is so tough, it's sooo tiring . None of the advice can even conceal this. I am stronger than you think. That's why I am still alive. But yes, crying alone everyday...

~A girl still living( from 20)

Hello. I never wanted to write the pain I went through on such a platform. But after seeing the post of #2153 I had to write this. 
 I'm not from Kuet. But the man I'm writing about is. 
Today I'll be writing about the most painful phase of my life that I faced. I fell in love with a guy. He was everything a woman can ask for. I told him I'm scared of relationships and commitments and I only want something real. He assured me a future and we continued talking. I told him I didn't want a fling because attachments are scary. And if one-day I'll loose him I wouldn't be able to bear it. He assured me with everything he could. He told me he wanted to marry me.  He loved me passionately. He did everything that a women could dream about. 
Time went by And then one day just like that he told me I meant nothing to him. He loves someone else. And has been in a relationship with another girl for a long time while being with me. It has been 2 years since. I'm not recovered. The trauma the pain just doesn't go away. maybe it never will. I told him everything I was scared of and he did that to me. He's changed multiple women till now. He's not committed to anyone. Just passes his time. After reading #2153 I just want to tell you brother. Why are you guys like this ? Why do do this to another soul? Sometimes this amusement of yours playing around with multiple women becomes a lifetime trauma for some. Please have fear in Allah please 

~anonymous

text:
#2163 
replied to #2154,

If you start studying, within 5 minutes you pick up your phone, and once you go online, you can't put it down until 2-3 hours have passed. Then you feel a lot of guilt, thinking, "Oh no! I wasted so much time." After that, you try to study again. But within 10 minutes, you feel the urge to grab your phone again. And once you pick it up, another half an hour or even 1-2 hours are gone.

Want a solution to this?

Have you heard about a strategy from Elon Musk? It's something I follow, and I believe it will help you increase your concentration while studying. In an interview, Elon Musk was once asked how he manages to do so much work alone and whether he faces any issues with concentration. Musk replied that he follows a strategy called **"Time Blocking."** The idea is to divide your time into small segments, or "blocks," and dedicate each block to a specific task. This approach significantly improves focus on your work.

You can apply this technique to your studies as well. Follow Elon Musk's time-blocking method, and you'll see that you no longer waste your focus or time. For example, divide your day into several blocks, with each block being 1 hour long. No matter what you're studying, don't take your eyes off the book until the 1-hour block is complete. By following this method, you can easily achieve 5-6 hours of focused study every day.

~someone from MSE 20

text:
#2161
Not everyone enjoys sarcasm. But a post or a sentence whether is sarcastic or is serious (on topics like sags, marriage, etc.), and someone failing to understand the tone jumps into the comments dropping serious moral lectures, even dragging the narrator’s family into it — who exactly are you trying to impress? 
Let's not even talk about your common sense, but at the very least, understand that you're actually insecure about your own morals and family orientation.
Being insecure isn’t a crime, but it is unhealthy. So, what you need to do to overcome this insecurity is to stay calm, not feel obligated to respond to everything everywhere, and allow others to live, think — even joke — in their own way, as long as it doesn’t directly affect you. Taking it upon yourself to correct everyone’s behavior unsolicited is not really a sign of gentleman.
If you take so much on your shoulders, you won’t have the strength left to lift her legs onto them. আর ঠ্যাং কাঁধে না উঠালে কিন্তু বাচ্চা হবে না বলে দিলাম।

[বি: দ্র: যেহেতু কুয়েট হুইস্পার্স ঠ্যাং কাঁধে তোলার পোস্ট দিয়েছে তাই এটা পশ্চিমা সংস্কৃতির প্রোমোটার। আসুন আমরা এই ইজরায়েলি হুইস্পার্সকে বয়কট করি]

~anonymous

text:
#2159
I’ve recently developed deep feelings for a girl I met online. She’s four years younger than me and not from KUET. Although our conversations were brief, something about her truly captured my heart. She’s kind, principled, and incredibly genuine qualities that aren’t easy to find.

Early on, she told me she doesn’t feel comfortable talking to strangers and gave me a clear boundary—a deadline, of sorts—saying I could share whatever I wanted before she cut off contact. After that, she stopped responding. I tried to reach out again, respectfully, hoping we could stay in touch, but things became shaky. Eventually, she blocked me from all platforms. I still have her phone number, but she made it clear she doesn’t engage with unfamiliar men—and from what I’ve learned, she sincerely lives by that standard.

I’m currently employed, stable, and was genuinely considering something serious—perhaps even asking for her hand in marriage. But before I could take any proper steps, things fell apart.

Right now, I’m struggling. I feel heartbroken—not in a fleeting, emotional way—but in a quiet, heavy, deeply personal way. This isn’t just a crush. It feels like something real slipped away before it even had the chance to begin. I’m starting to wonder if I’ll ever be able to open up to someone again.

I’m sharing this not out of weakness, but out of honesty. If anyone has advice or guidance, I’d be truly grateful. Sometimes, even the strongest of us need a hand to hold—or simply a voice that says, “You’ll be okay.”

Please. admin post this at your convenience.

~anonymous

Back then, during my school days, I used to play video games on my mom’s phone. It was like a competition among the people like me and my friends who had the most collections of games. I have neither any PC nor wifi connections. But I was having fun playing with that device. After coming back from my school, it was the best part of the day. Now I have my own PC and the best possible smartphone, but why aren’t those games funny anymore? They still exist, but somehow I abandoned them without even knowing the exact time. Why do our tastes change just like people who have been living/suffering or probably sharing  their best moments have walked away from us at some point in life?

#2150
Big thanks to my honorable seniors for all the kind words and advice on my last post (#2127). 

Anyway… today I saw a post here about long-distance relationships and got reminded of a video I watched recently. The guy in it straight-up said,
“If one cracks a public university and the other doesn’t—99.99% break up confirmed.”
I paused the video. Rewound. Watched again. 

Now every time I see a couple where one’s already in public university and the other’s still trying… I start hearing breakup background music. 

But for real, does getting into a public university make someone emotionally upgrade to “higher version” and forget their old patch? Or is it just the stress, distance, and new people that mess it up?

I’m not in a relationship, but seeing this drama unfold around me got me thinking... is love actually that fragile? Or do people just change too fast once they get that university tag?

~[KUET_Sphinx_M24]

Let me make a true confession. Do you know what fear really is? Fear is thinking about your family. It's the realization that you can’t always follow your own will—you can’t freely choose your path or take a stand, even when you know it’s right. Why? Because your parents are waiting for you. If anything ever happens to you, they would lose everything. And the truth is, no one else would care or take responsibility. Who would look after them if I were gone? That question haunts me. Even when I know what’s right, I can’t be with that right. Fear wraps around me in these thoughts and holds me back. That’s my truth.
I think who have nothing to lose, is the strongest one!

~A man who has nothing, yet everything.

text:
#2130
(Media Attachment: https://pbs.twimg.com/media/CwwRs1MWIAAgIvR.jpg)
A footnote to #2124
To my juniors of 2k24, I embrace all of you with love that you have proved to be competent and hard working as I was in my hay day. But let me tell you my story of the horrors of public university. I am the youngest in a family of 4 siblings. All of us got into public university. The ragging situation was so bad during the last time 'they' got into power that I am told, me being beaten for hours by an entire regiment of BCL hooligans inside KUET was a far better scenario compared to what they saw in BUET, DU. I scoffed off the notion on the basis that they are trying to undermine my feats of endurance (lol proud to be alive). But the thing is, Awami League was a professional organization, with professional standards and ethics akin to an international human trafficking organization. In Engineering, great emphasis is given to teach the "Survivor Bias". When we see the failures of Awami League, we ourselves fail to see their silent successes. They had the professional propensity to dissolve a lot of issues without causing drama; issues which, you will soon learn, are going to be nightmare fuel if 'they' get their way. I'm not saying that Awami League is great, we need Awami League back. We don't want them back ever again. Unlike South Park, we are not choosing to vote between a TURD SANDWICH AND A GIANT DOUCHEBAG. We want the better alternative, something fresh and fair, something that spells 'welfare state'. Let's say if we let 'them' come to campus, surely 17 years of inactivity requires some warm up to work around the rusts and jams, corrosions are not cheap at all. They take time and money to mend. But that time and money are gonna come out first from the pockets of 2k24. So my quiet whispers (no pun intended) are as follows

"Cancel Your Admission, Or swim in Khajar Pookoor!"

I love you guys, you're our future. I love my country and my juniors.

~The Wounded Revolutionary

#2127
I’ve been admitted to a department in KUET’s 2024 batch. But lately, I’ve been noticing a lot of people on Messenger feeling depressed about their university and subject choice. Some are regretting not getting into other places, thinking things would’ve been better elsewhere. People are already worried about which subject will be in demand after 4 years, whether they’ll even find a job in Bangladesh, and so on.

And this is all before classes have even started.

It makes me wonder — will this depression get worse once classes begin? Looking at KUET Whispers all day, it seems like even those who weren’t initially depressed end up falling into it after going there. Does it really happen that everyone changes like that?

I used to imagine KUET as this amazing place where people are chill, where I’d enjoy campus life. But seeing all this now, even before stepping in. And watching this stuff all day long is starting to make me doubt myself too.

Please note that I will now be posting under the name “KUET_Sphinx_M24” on KUET Whispers. I would appreciate it if you could refrain from using this handle.

~KUET_Sphinx_M24

text:
#2114
I haven’t really made many friends in university. Everyone just kind of knows of me, that’s all. I often notice how others enjoy spending time with their friends, how happy they seem, how they post stories. And then I wonder why can’t I be like that? Why am I not happy? At the end of the day, I just feel alone. There’s no one at KUET who truly listens to me without judgment, who accepts me completely someone who won’t find faults in everything I do. 

I see other girls laughing, hanging out with groups of guys, having fun and they seem so happy. But I’m not like that. Is that why I’m not happy? Should I have made more friends? Or is it true that when people find new friends, they forget the one who stood by them in their loneliest moments?

~Someone who makes everyone laugh but has no one to bring a smile to that person.

text:
#2076
What is loneliness?
People often think it means being alone.
But it’s not.
Sometimes, it’s having too many people around—too many messages, too many notifications, too many voices and still not knowing who to talk to or what to say.

My inbox? Full.
Friends. Family.
Some messages are important, some completely random, and some hit deep, emotional stuff.
I know they care. I do.
But still, I stare at the screen and… nothing.
I can’t figure out what to reply, or even if I want to.

It’s strange,,,,wanting to talk so badly,
craving connection,
yet ignoring calls when they come in.
Not because I don’t want to talk,
but because I don’t know how.
Or maybe I just feel like no one will truly get it.

I’ve got people.
I’ve got options.
I’ve got distractions that should keep me busy,,,
but inside,,, there’s this heaviness.
This constant emptiness.
Nothing hits right.
Not my phone. Not movies. Not even books,,,,and I used to love reading.

It’s not exactly depression… or maybe it is, I don’t even know.
It’s just this quiet sadness that sticks with me all day.
And the worst part?
I don’t even know why I’m like this.
Because this isn’t me,,,,not the real me.

But one thing I’m sure of- 
I don’t want noise.
I don’t want fake connections or more content on my feed.
I just want one real moment.
A deep conversation.
Something raw, about life, about philosophy, about everything and nothing.
I want to talk until it makes sense or even if it doesn’t.
I just want to feel seen.
Heard.
Understood.
To stop pretending I’m okay when I’m clearly not.

All I want is that just one real connection.
But here’s the irony:
I want to connect, and yet, at the same time… I don’t.
I’m tired. I can’t keep up with this anymore.

Maybe I know what I should do.
Maybe it’s time to cut off the noise.
Disconnect for a bit.
Stay off social media.
Spend time in silence, with my own thoughts.
Just think. Observe. Write.
Get lost in my own imagination.
Or… maybe I don’t know.

Maybe I’ll disappear for a while after this post.
Or maybe I won’t.
But whatever happens—just pray for me.
I think I need that right now.

-Perhaps you know who or not (EEE'23) 
Loneliness eats my soul.

text:
#2075
No one really knows where the education system of Bangladesh is going. When the ""Creative Question Method"" was first introduced in 2010, it was meant to improve critical thinking. But in practice, it became the starting point of confusion for both students and teachers. A 2022 study by the Education Watch group reported that over 60% of teachers in secondary schools lacked proper training to implement the creative system effectively.

Since then, the system has gone through frequent reforms. From 2010 to 2025, Bangladesh has changed or revised its national curriculum at least 5 times — each time without allowing enough adjustment time for educators. According to Transparency International Bangladesh (TIB), 85% of secondary school teachers feel inadequately trained for new curriculum models like the recently introduced Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) in 2023.

Meanwhile, the younger generation’s attention span is shrinking. With the rise of platforms like TikTok and Instagram Reels, students are spending more time on their phones than in their textbooks. A 2022 LightCastle Partners survey shows that over 85% of Gen Z in Bangladesh spend more than 4 hours a day on mobile devices, mostly on entertainment. Academic stress has become lighter in many cases, but instead of turning to productive learning, students are becoming more digitally addicted.

Now, everyone is demanding change, but no one is willing to give the system the time it needs to stabilize. Everyone claims their own logic is right, but there's no unified vision for reform. The result? An education system that’s failing to teach critical thinking, real-world skills, or adaptability.

At the university level, the situation looks even bleaker. Instead of thinking about contributing to the country, most students dream of settling abroad. According to the International Organization for Migration (IOM), over 12,000 Bangladeshi students leave for higher education abroad every year, and less than 30% of them return. A 2023 Brac University survey found that 71% of final-year students have no long-term plans to stay in Bangladesh.

So, when students are disengaged, teachers are unprepared, and the best minds plan to leave — the question remains: what future does this country really have?

~Munem Shahriar Onim(EEE'23)

text:
#2069
I once believed love meant giving everything. And when I found her, I gave it all—my thoughts, my dreams, my future. She became the center of my universe, the reason I smiled, the reason I wanted to be better. I felt I could do anything—become anyone—just to match the version she dreamed of.

But one day, she said something that shook me:
"If you love too much, the importance of love will fade. The spark might die."

It echoed in my mind like a question with no answer. How could something so pure lose its meaning just by being intense? Was I wrong to love her deeply? Or was she afraid of love that burns too bright?

Now I sit between two truths—my love that wants to give her everything, and her words that ask for space, for balance. Maybe love isn’t just about how much you give, but how gently you give it.

~anonymous

~ Someone who's still learning to let go

text:
#2057
To the one I never truly knew,

You were just a quiet presence in the distance— someone who once cared for me in silence.

I don’t know why you’re still in my mind, or why I’m even writing this; maybe because I ain't able to say out loud. But now, in your absence, I feel something I can’t quite name— a strange stillness, a quiet sorrow… like the echo of a song I never got to hear.

You left without warning. And with you, left all the unspoken things— your silent kindness, your quiet hope, and the gentle love you may have once held.

Maybe it wasn’t love. Maybe it was something unfinished— a story that never got the chance to begin.

I don’t know why this hurts, but it does.

Wherever you are, I hope you’ve found peace.

And if your heart ever ached in silence— I want you to know: it didn’t go unseen. Not forever. I just didn’t want to give an illusory hope that might later turns into regret.

If your heart ever waited for mine— I’m sorry it waited alone.

~From the one , 
you never truly knew.

text:
#2053
I used to see posts saying, “KUET takes away everything and gives nothing,” and wonder—how could that be? How could something we cherished so deeply become someone else’s source of pain?

After orientation, I felt like the happiest girl alive. I had everything—my trio of besties, classes, exams, excitement; a routine that ended with coming home to do all the silly things. Endless laughter, and the warmth of talking to my mom every hour—it brought a kind of peace I can’t even describe. Life felt perfect. KUET truly felt like a second home. Sure, there were small cracks—but who doesn’t have those?

But maybe it was too perfect to last.

And then? Yes, it happened. Everything changed. In just one week—only seven days.

I won’t share the details—but now I understand.

Life can flip in the blink of an eye. You never see it coming.

~Just someone who gave life a second chance… and lost again

text:
#2046
Some people on campus who are truly alone. For one reason or another, they don’t have close friends or partners and even if they do, those friendships or relationships are in long distance or their friends don''t have time for them. At the end of the day, I feel like we don’t always need friends, but it’s nice to have someone to hang out with like going to a restaurant or street food or sitting by the river.

The problem, especially for girls, is that going out alone doesn’t always feel safe. Even if nothing bad happens, there’s still that uncomfortable feeling. I wish there was an app like Tinder, but not for dating. Just something to match people who want to hang out for a day. No relationships, no commitments, not even deep friendship just a chill one-day plan to ease the mental stress.

It could even match two girls or two guys based on vibe, not gender or anything romantic. For people like us who feel alone sometimes, something like this would actually be really helpful.

~anonymous

text:
#2042
Not sure if I’m too old for this page, but posting anyway. Seen a lot of heavy posts lately, so here’s something a bit lighter.
Got married this year to my "uni sweetheart" after years of dating. 
This morning he was so late for work that almost forgot his belt, didn’t even tuck his shirt in, still made time to kiss me on the forehead before running out.

Last week, I was the one who was drowning in work and came home completely exhausted almost everyday. He cooked dinner, washed the dishes, did our laundry, cleaned the house, and still gave me a foot massage because “you looked like you needed it.”
People say love fades eventually, but it doesn't have to. Six years have passed still showing up for each other like it’s day one.
So ladies, don't settle for less. 

~anonymous

text:
#2041
To #2031
I might be wrong... maybe I just thought it was you. But something about the songs and the timing felt too familiar. If it really was you, I don’t know why nothing was said. I’m not angry—just a little confused. Maybe I expected too much. Maybe I cared a bit more than I should’ve.
There was a time I wished you were mine. Not in a dramatic way—just a quiet hope that stayed with me. And when that didn’t happen, I still stayed. I told myself being near you was enough, even if it hurt a little. But after a while, I started to feel like I didn’t belong there anymore. Like even my friendship wasn’t needed. So I chose to walk away—not out of anger, but for peace. I needed to stop breaking my own heart.
And even when I tried to sort everything out—just as friends—I still ended up feeling the same again. Like even that… even just being there quietly, as a friend, wasn’t really needed. I tried to tell myself it was enough. That I was okay with it. But deep down, I think I was just hoping to matter. In some small way. And maybe I didn’t.

~anonymous

text:
#2035
It breaks my heart in ways I can’t explain. Seeing you wear matching colors with that beautiful girl in the same Panjabi you once wore when we matched.
Back when it was us. Back when I still had you.

How did something so sacred turn into a memory I now cry over?
I miss you deeply, painfully, endlessly. You were the best thing that ever happened to me. I loved you with everything I had. I still do.
And I know in some corner of my soul, I always will.

God… I’m so cooked. So wrecked.
You have no idea how hard it’s been.

How am I supposed to move on from someone like you? Every time I talk to someone, I find myself comparing.
Every laugh, every text, every moment, 
It’s always you I’m measuring them against.
And then, just like that, I lose interest. Because no one feels like you. No one is you.

Still… even after all this ache, I fold my hands and whisper your name in my prayers. I ask God to keep you happy. To keep you healthy.
Even if it’s not with me.

Because loving you has always been more about your smile than my own.

~anonymous

I loved you with everything I had. I still do. And I know in some corner of my soul, I always will.  God… I’m so cooked. So wrecked. You have no idea how hard it’s been.  How am I supposed to move on from someone like you? Every time I talk to someone, I find myself comparing. Every laugh, every text, every moment,  It’s always you I’m measuring them against. And then, just like that, I lose interest. Because no one feels like you. No one is you.  Still… even after all this ache, I fold my hands and whisper your name in my prayers. I ask God to keep you happy. To keep you healthy. Even if it’s not with me.  Because loving you has always been more about your smile than my own.

text:
#2034
For a complete 12 years, I was kind of a nerd—topped almost every exam. There's a secret about my past love that I’ve never told anyone. Actually, I can't share this with any of my friends, so I’m sharing it here…

There was a teacher who used to teach me maths at a local coaching center. His eyes were a trap—I would literally get lost in them during every class. I knew there was no future between us, but still, I kept infatuating over him. He was also a teacher at my college, so being together was impossible.

But my hopeless romantic heart never cared. Even knowing the truth, it kept dreaming—dreaming about us, even about our wedding. I used to stare into his eyes, and it felt like there was no end. I could sit there and just soak in his gaze forever.

Eventually, my parents found out when I was going through my admission process. They scolded me in such a traumatizing way that the memory still haunts me and fills me with immense guilt. I remember doing maths with a severely aching heart and trembling hands after they pulled me out of his coaching during my medical entrance preparation.

It’s been two years, and I still haven’t found someone new. My heart still yearns for the kind of obsession I had for him. I was never in a relationship, so this was the peak of my love life. The saddest part is—when he found out about my feelings, he indirectly asked for my hand once... and within a month, he married someone else.

That’s how it ended.

And I guess the 19th love theory about a girl is true. The way I felt for him—I haven’t been able to feel for anyone else. He broke me without ever touching me, and here I am, still single. All these memories came flooding back today because I saw him again on the road.

Maybe, in another life, I’ll be your life partner.

~anonymous 22

text:
#2031
You once said, "appreciate the positiveness." Do you know how many times I've felt like a loser? These thoughts keep haunting me....every time, even when I close my eyes. And somehow, even in sleep, you find your way back.

I don’t even know why I’m writing this here. Maybe I’m too shy… or maybe I just don’t want to bother you anymore. So I am leaving this here, to let this letter get buried beneath the posts—like a message in a bottle, drifting away, lost in the sea of time.

Hey… if I tell you that I need you? Ah, it doesn't matter. Nothing will happen. Nothing ever does.

Why couldn’t you understand why I messaged you in the first place? Why wasn’t I straightforward? Why did I send the letter and song through sendthesong.xyz website on February 1st? Why not just directly to your inbox?

Why am I so good at losing my favorite people and the things I care about? Why am I always the one watching them walk away, silently, as if I never mattered?

So many questions... 
Circling
Haunting me
Forever

The Unsent Letter
14 April, 2025

~anonymous

text:
#2029
There is a video of Zia Mohiuddin where he talks about regret. He says he once loved a girl. But never told her. Fate came in between them, then time, and age. 40-45 years later they met in London by chance and he said ‘ek jamana Tha jab aap pe bahaut dil tha.’ She said, ‘apne batay kiu nehi’. He said these smilingly but you could wear the Everest, but every could see the pain.

I think of this every now and then when I can’t manage to fall asleep. I have made myself turn away from you telling me it’s hard, that you are this beautiful angel and I am something entirely unmatched for you so much so that it may be regarded as sin (A friend actually told me this). There is also religion and many other barriers. 

I sometimes think the world between us was made just to push us away with forms, ideologies, color and misfortune. I can’t explain how precious you are. Almost like seeing a world learning to bloom a flower after centuries. I am not sad. It more concerning than that. I think I’ll carry this incurable scar like an animal. Like the original sin that Christians believe in, that they, who were born after 2000 years after the death of Christ, is to blame for his crucifixion. I was born with the sin to be forever a stranger to you.

One day, I wanna hear you say to me the crudest things possible. Maybe that would be enough to cover my heart to stop spilling blood. In hope that it wouldn’t draw your initials on my notebook, HC.

~anonymous

How many nights must pass, how many days must we endure, how many months must go by, and how many more lives must be shattered before you finally understand? It’s not just a matter of authority anymore. It’s a matter of conscience.

1. They say they need a loyal partner like KUET VC

- They haven't seen, felt my special one yet. Her loyalty is so PRECIOUS and Special to me that I will never share how it feels to anyone except her!

2. They say they need a caring partner like KUET to send email every now and then and even send SMS to parents for our well being (I mean that's what KUET wanted to show right)

- They haven't seen, felt my special one yet. My special one wouldn't let even a single day or night pass without asking me indirectly (trust me, we never asked, how u doing or Kemon aso in our 2+ years being tog, our one text somehow informs others what the other person is facing) how I am doing, even when we are having the worst misunderstanding of all time, would never go sleep without a GN hug during even that phase.

3. Some say KUET takes aways our closest ones

- They haven't seen/felt my special one yet. KUET and her uni did make a distance between us, shortened our time but still, it's brought us even closer. I would never know how precious she is unless I was here in KUET, in extreme pressure, she was too in her uni but that pressure stick us tog, not split us. One tight hug for 20 mins is enough to recover from all the stress and pressure.

Now, the following is for my special one, I know you're reading this cuz I shared this page with you and you knew this was coming:
How dare you page audience think I'll share something, only for her, with you?? We have DM. She's prescious okay, and I'm the jhinuk that protects the mukta(which is she), don't expect me to reveal our precious moments with you!

btw, special one, you complain about not recognizing your voice right? Well that's justified, after all, that's one of the ways to see your cutie ovimani face when we don't have a misunderstanding for weeks yk. But you do know I always understand when you're mad, sad, happy or even when you're having those days by just hearing one sentence from my most precious voice, right?

Oh a thing for you all, we live almost hundred of thousands meters apart, only met twice offline and never met each other and saw each for the first 20+ months.

An anniversary gift for her? This post is more valuable, memorable and precious to her then anything, trust me, I know her better than she knows herself.

You want same kind of her/him in your life? Get married. Period.

text:
#2014
Replying to #1995

Sister, come back to reality. The person who didn't even choose you to be the mother of his child would have been your best support system during your labor pain — get out of that delusional belief.

Do you know why I’m able to say something so harsh? Because I’ve felt this emotion myself. During my elder sister’s ""firani"" event, my mother didn’t rest for even a moment in those four days. She was entirely focused on what my brother-in-law liked to eat, what he wanted to taste — just busy with that. On the day my sister was leaving, all the elderly women in my family were making various traditional pithas fot her in-laws’ house. I was in charge of packing those pithas.

That whole time, I was both happy and sad. My sister was really enjoying seeing her husband being pampered like a beloved son-in-law. Her joy made me happy too. But at the same time, it broke my heart thinking that someone I loved could have received this same "jamai ador" from my family, yet he didn’t even choose that path. Just like you, I couldn’t hold back my tears that moment.

He loves to eat, and my mother loves feeding people. Every time he would visit, my mother would cook all his favorite dishes. And when he leaves, she’d definitely pack different types of pithas and snacks for him to take. But now, all of that feels like a third conditional sentence in English grammar— something unreal, something that didn’t happen and never will.

For a long time, I grieved over it. Now I’ve accepted the truth. Someone who didn’t even choose my mother to be his mother-in-law will obviously never receive the love of a cherished son-in-law from her. Me grieving over that is just foolish. You should accept reality too, dear. May Allah heal your soul. Ameen.

~anonymous

text:
#2011
I write this with a heavy heart, gripped by fear — a deep, unsettling fear. You all know what we’ve done, and what has been done to us. The past days have weighed on me more than words can say.

I don’t know what will happen to me today or tomorrow or the next day. I don’t know what fate awaits my friends either. The laughter and joy of campus life now feel like a distant, fading memory — a dream I can barely reach for. I don’t want to live in fear. I don’t want to carry this anger. Is that too much to ask?

We have come so far, fought so hard. I just hope the pain, the blood, the struggle — that none of it was in vain. Not long ago, just two years back, I arrived in this unfamiliar place. And today, I stand ready to claim my right to belong here.

These past two months have changed me. They've made me grow in ways I never expected. I now feel emotions I never knew I was capable of. I’ve never known hatred before — but now I do. And I hate that I feel this way. That hatred has taken something from me, something I may never get back.

But what frightens me most is the thought of living under his rule, of a life controlled and silenced. That is a future I cannot accept.

May the Lord be with us.

~anonymous

#2008
You’re just like my father. How can a person be the fully copy of another person ? But you’re. I’ve heard that girls want their love to be like their father. But in my case it was never that case. I always wanted that if I ever like anyone , Allah do not make him like my father. But you turned out exactly like him. Do you know how hard it was for me when I was fighting with myself? I used to tell myself I’m not a strong woman like my mother so I won’t be able to handle you. No matter how much I tried to push you away yet you didn’t give up. Maybe you knew that I like you. But when I felt like that I should give you a chance and accepted that I’ve the same feelings for you , you walked away. You’re so selfish like him. You put every blame on me like him. text:
#1999
Writing it here, hope you’ll see as it’s one of your favourite pages. 

Do you remember 3 years back, you once told me you’ll pay me if I write a fake confession about you somewhere and I said I would rather die. Look at the irony of fate, here I am writing about you and us in-front of thousands of strangers.

it all started with a song from our mutual favourite band. A comment on a random page, a friend request, a reply to fb story. In a world full of co- incidences, I’ll call it fate. Two angst teenagers who sought refuge from the chaos of world and found comfort in each other suddenly. 

From being strangers to academic rivals to best friends to lovers, you were my first everything. Soon we fell in love. Shared spotify playlists, poems, letters our love was marked everywhere. Writing our names in bench and even nicknames we once mocked seemed cute.

Then came, the breaking point every couple faces. Distance between two cities, constant misunderstandings, trust issues and people around making us believe we deserve a lot better, slowly tarnished our love. 

We broke up thinking maybe we really deserve a lot better. A better looking, more compatible or with same ideologies. Time moved as fast as a thunderbolt and so did people in their lives. But only we couldn’t. May it be the loneliest nights or the busiest days, our hearts would always long for the other. Not being able to understand where it went wrong. But guilt and traumas kept reminding that it’s better to leave. So, we never really gave us a chance yet memories of past lingered like smell of an old perfume. Why the word “forget” doesn’t work for us we couldn’t figure out.

We tried to meet others but our heart felt like a punctured vessel that one tries to fill forever with water but it always dries out. Hundreds of closures to each other but we couldn’t cut contact. We left traces of our love in mails, posts or relating to any song or show that comes up. Quoting that song from our favourite band as it relates, 

“Maybe I'm too busy being yours 
         to fall for somebody new
  Now I've thought it through
         Crawling back to you”

Here we are again after one year, failed to convince ourselves we can forget. Here we are loving each other like we are sixteen. 
I love you. The same I once did. And I would choose you every single day.

~Anonymous

text:
#1998
Love, in a way, has the same fate as a cigarette.

You know the ending, but you still choose this way to suffer. Out of desire, out of habit. You light it up, watch the smoke curl, take a puff, and you inhale it, savoring the moment while the toxins slowly seep into your tongue and proceed towards the lungs, their destination.

With every exhale, you trade a moment of your lifespan, yet you do it gladly, because the moment of pleasure is greater than the unpredictable spiteful future.

Slowly, as these thoughts unwind, your cigarette comes to an end. All that is left are mere ashes. You watch them while rewinding the heavenly moment now gone.

Love comes, and we feel like our hearts are on fire. That longing, that adrenaline rush, the uncertainty of it, makes us burn inside. But we all enjoy it, don’t we?

We enjoy the moments-the hours of conversation, future planning, knowing each other, getting along so well like we are two sides of a coin, ignoring the inevitable truth that those times will fade like smoke leaving behind a scar no remedy can cure.

But what is even the future without the one we long for? So, despite the fights, chaos, complications, and so on, we hold onto each other for the sake of love. In the process, our love too comes to an end.

We think there’s still time, but it slips away and all that is left is memories, which we replay in our heads as we whisper to ourselves, 

 “Hold on and hope
that we’ll find our way 
back in the end.”

~anonymous

text:
#1995
2 days back my sister had a baby Alhamdulillah . But she wanted to have a normal delivery instead of c - section. So while she was having contractions she was screaming a lottttt and bhaiya could hear it from outside panicking what’s going on. After sometime she asked for him. The moment she saw him she started crying and they hugged each other and both were crying. Bhaiya also cried. Idk why for a moment out of nowhere your face came to my mind and I started thinking if I were in this position nobody could have taken care of me other than you. But reality hit hard quickly , you don’t want me. You’ve moved on . I also started crying. I was crying and helping her and was trying my best so that nobody sees me crying. Then when there was nobody I cried at the hospital in front of my mother making her as a shield. She asked me why I was crying like that , told her it was because of apuni. After that I continuously splashed water on my face so that I won’t cry anymore , but it just  didn’t stop . 
Why all on a sudden you came to my mind and why can’t I move on when you’ve moved on so easily ? Why ? Why ? Why ? 
While returning home , I tried my bestest not to cry. Then I went to have a shower , cried again like a baby with the shower on so that nobody could hear me. It hurts. Wanted to text you so badly , and wanted ask you : how can you sleep so soundly when I’m wide awake 
but couldn’t cause I know nothing will happen. If you’ve liked me you’d have taken actions already. But your silence says everything. It’s not like I don’t have any other options , trust me I do. If you’ve 10 , then Ive 100 . It’s just about choices. You chose to go for another person and I didn’t. 

~anonymous

text:
#1991
On behalf of the people in Gaza who are currently suffering, I know it won't be much, but to help them, we must stop using products like Coca-Cola, KFC, Bata, Sprite, Fanta, other Unilever products, or Nestlé, etc. But we are also greatly dependent on them. Again, we do have alternate products in our country, but they are not as good as the ones we import from American or Israeli companies. So I was thinking, since we all students are uniting together, why not all of us create one large company with the help of all the senior and junior students of our country to create our own products, so that we don’t have to depend on Israeli products?

I am not saying to build our own Unilever or Nestlé, because that would be foolish, but we can start small—like an alternative drinking water company. This means an alternative to Kinley, Aquafina, etc. We might already have alternatives for drinking water, but due to lack of availability and high production cost, we are getting humbled. So, with the help of all the students and teachers of all universities, schools, and colleges, if they help us with funding, we might be able to create an alternative company. And slowly, if it shows great results, we can take the next step by producing products like body lotion, soaps, shampoo, cosmetics, or snacks like chips, soda, chocolate bars, etc. This way, we won’t have to depend on Israel anymore.

Think about it—if all students in this protest start to build and sell their own products on campus, we can successfully replace Israeli products. And if all students just invest a minimum of 10 TK, it will be more than enough for us to create a company of our own. Soon, we will be able to replace any Israeli products.

It was my opinion, by the way. I hope we can start from KUET. If the seniors of KUET guide and help us, we might be able to do something for the people of Gaza this time.

~anonymous

text:
From the River to the Sea, Pal**tine Will Be Free

From the river’s banks to the endless sea,
A land of hope, of longing, and of plea.
Beneath the sky, the olive trees still grow,
Their roots in the soil where ancient rivers flow.

Through tear-streaked eyes, the children dream of peace,
A future where their suffering will cease.
The call for justice rises from the sand,
A steadfast wish, a voice across the land.

From every hill, from every humble street,
Pal**tine’s heart beats strong, and it won’t retreat.
Even if Ga*a is erased from the map,
The spirit of Pal**tine will never collapse.

Nature will rise, and Allah’s justice will come,
The devils of Isr**l will pay for what they’ve done.
Through fire and blood, through years of endless strife,
Pal**tine’s soul will endure, for it is life.

text:
#1978
To my beloved,
Im a fool. To think I could match my words with how i feel. To even try to express what I see,  You.
The grace of a shrine maiden, flowing like the summer breeze. That gentleness in your eyes could calm a raging storm.
Voice soft as a whispering Sheuli petal, yet it echoes in my heart. O how I felt, seeing You for the first time, Moonlight making a ripple on still water, dilating time itself. Looking at your eyes was a fatality and Im fallen ever since. 
Yet I never got the courage to say anything. 
My beloved, Im a fool. The weight on my chest making its hard to breathe , yet I wont dare to interfere with something so gentle, so precious and delicate, You.
As one could have several versions of himself on others mind, I wonder, how I resides in your world, maybe as a monster, could be non-existent. well, that's fine. Meeting the expectation of a goddess is beyond my reach. Though,  I wish. I wish the possible timeline I dreamt of would collide with this world, causing the unexpected, Paradox where I can hold your hands. Where I can make you smile for the rest of my life, have you as mine.
My beloved, I wish I weren't a fool. Wish I were blind...

~Anonymous f

text:
#1969
1st April 2024 — I didn’t notice the date.
When he said he met his ex again and told me so many things, I couldn’t take it.
When I finally saw the date, I still tried to believe it was just an April Fool’s joke.
Because neither is he going back to his ex—though he loves her a lot, she has someone newNor is he coming to me. Instead, he behaved badly with me and told me not to talk to him.I was... lost.
The day passed.So many things happened.We had gone into a relationship. We loved each other.A very beautiful story.
He was broken by his ex, and we fixed everything together.

1st April 2025 —
We don’t talk.He loves someone else now.They’re in a relationship—making moments, meeting...
And me?
I am gifted that 1st April,2024 situation of his..I’m left with the memory of 1st April 2024, the day he poured out his broken story.
A heartbreak story of 9 months for me. He managed to set up everything in just 6 months.
But what about me?
Every day, I’m drowning... deeper and deeper.

~A broken heart moving closer to death,
not with noise,
but with the quiet surrender
of someone who once believed
love could save.

Sometimes, I really wonder if you miss me or not—if I ever cross your mind. And if I do, then why don’t you reach out? Because I’m not going to knock on your door anymore, no matter what happens. I want to move on because you’ve moved on.

You never gave me a reason. You just disappeared.

I miss the old me—the one who loved herself more than anyone, who was confident in herself. Now, I kind of hate myself because I can’t bring myself to hate you. And still... I miss you.

text:
#1938
Struggle with Faith 

Since childhood I have struggled with my faith, surprisingly it's not because of my obsession with science from an young age. 

For some reason I have always been able to separate science from religion, and for me religion was never about being right, or proving that ""I am right about the world, and you are wrong."" It has always been about teachings of religion, virtue, community, faith in something bigger and above all Hope. 

But around mid '10s I began to learn about the cruelties of the world, the wars, the famines, malnourished children and so much more... The first thing that really shook my faith was the War in Yemen... Those children... You can't convince me that there is any justifiable reason for God to allow that to happen to children whose only fault was being born in the wrong place at the wrong time... You may want give some explaintion but no need, I have heard them all and find none of them convincing. People don't even talk about Yemen anymore... The love that makes sense—the one rooted in compatibility, shared values, and a smooth rhythm—feels safe and enduring. It’s the kind of love that builds a strong foundation, one where you can grow together without constant turbulence.

Then there’s the love that makes no sense—the one that shakes your soul, defies logic, and sometimes even disrupts your life. It’s intoxicating, unpredictable, and often fleeting, yet it leaves an imprint so deep that it lingers even when it’s gone.

If you're lucky, you might find both in one person—but if not, you have to decide what your heart can live with and what it can live without.Which one should we choose?

~anonymous

#1926
Whenever I see the person at peace with that present for whom I am "EX", I glance at the mirror—only to face the ruin I’ve become. Once nurtured by my mother’s care, now frail, eyes sunken, hair falling, sickness clinging to me. Time slips away, yet I remain stuck.

I tell myself to focus—on my career, my parents' happiness—but no matter how hard I try, I fail. Helplessness consumes me, followed by panic, breathlessness.

If leaving was inevitable, why was I first broken? Did anyone ever grasp this agony?

Why was I chosen just to be left in ruins?

Once, I was strong, driven—a fighter. Now, I am lost, weak, aimless. But does any of it even matter? And can a mere "sorry" ever fix it? 

So many will say that one day, I’ll laugh at my own words. But will my career ever be fixed? Will the damage ever be undone? Or is it already too late? Because truth be told, I’ve lost too much already.

~Someone who can't but always keep dreaming death, not to be in peace, just, can't take anymore.

text:
#1904
Fading into the abyss

You move through the world like a beam of light, never hesitating, never faltering. And I? I exist in the abyss, in the spaces between your moments, in the silence that lingers when you walk away. I don’t know if you ever thought of me, if I ever brushed against your thoughts, but I, oh, I have drowned in you.

There are things that belong to the light, and things that are meant to fade. Some exist to be seen, to be held, to be part of a story. Others remain unseen, drifting at the edges, watching, waiting for a moment that will never come.

So now, I am trying to dissolve into the shadows where I have always belonged, to let the abyss take me. To erase every part of me that ever reached for you.
But even as I sink, something of me lingers, a whisper in the wind, a flicker in the dark.

The presence that never mattered.

~Random pages from Cinderella's Diary
