text:
#1262
So there’s this girl from BME I really like. She’s prolly the most gorgeous girl my eyes had the luxury to lay upon. And I must admit, my desire for her must raise a shit ton of eyebrows, me being a somewhat average-looking dude. That’s why I am behind a curtain to save myself from humiliation. But you know what, if it hadn’t been for my poor luck, I could have at least been your friend Mummu if we met frequently in a warm environment, me being from another dept and constantly in angst. I know what I am capable of and what I can offer. I’m neither an introvert nor an extrovert. I’m an angsty guy who values people he likes. But forget all that. You are not a trophy to achieve. 

All I want to say is that I would cherish an opportunity to get to know you, like really know you, and be around you. And also to let me be expressed to you so that you know I can be a meaningful part of your life. But I neither have the grounds nor the hope that if I text you all this, I will ever succeed. I mean why would you be interested? I am someone you don’t know, and I don’t have the physical features to intrigue your biological programming. But allow me the dignity to enlighten you (btw, this text is not generated by AI, I am a piece of work)Maybe because I am very intriguing. I am a unique piece of shit in this shithole, you know why? Because I can do things very few people around you can and because I can see through my biology and understand what is what. Love, relationships, and dating are just veiled lust. May we go beyond that? Maybe we can try to understand each other like some conscious people intrigued enough to go beyond the norms that drown us. Just imagine, two people dating are always always looking for a way to get intimate with each other like some hungry cats. It's insulting to me that with everything that we are capable of, we limit ourselves to lust. 

So what if you give yourself the liberty to give each other a chance to sit and talk? I already expressed how I’m in awe of your elegance gifted by god. But as I said, intimacy comes way below everything else, only when we are completely helpless, we are human beings after all. So your beauty won’t be a symbol of intimacy for me. People have dreamt of paradise but no one managed to leave the scorched earth yet. Even if I could, why would I? The punishing Telighati gave me this sweet platform (Love you KUET whispers, you are a better wingman than Barney fr) to express my deep feelings for you that is not pragmatic. I mean, why would I want to leave where you live and breathe? What do you say, are you open to it? 

P.S. I can be irresistibly romantic too but I bypassed that because wouldn’t you be uncomfortable if a guy came to you all of a sudden and started throwing himself to you like some drops of mud or smth? I jus want us to help grow each other. The universe is too much of a bitch for humans to tolerate alone. 

-Somewhat anonymous

text:
#1247
Just a midnight thought:
Being in a relationship was never in your wildest dreams, but life is full of surprises, isn't it?
You meet a boy, both feel a spark, and you fall in love. The relationship is in full swing, and love has completely devoured both of you. But not long after, things aren't the same as they used to be.

One day, your boyfriend makes you feel like you're his one true soulmate and that he would do anything for you, but the very next day, he gives you nothing but anxiety and mental pressure. How would you explain that?

Everything was fine before I met him, but after meeting him, I genuinely felt like he was the missing piece of my life. Now, I often wonder: Was it my mistake to love someone without considering the consequences? Is it that I'm not putting in enough effort to understand his situation, or is the other person taking advantage of my emotions, knowing I can't leave him now?

Maybe this is the price we pay for loving a boy, not a man.

~anonymous

I don’t think I have any feelings for you anymore. Yet, there’s a part of me that still lingers in the past, where I yearned for you so desperately. It’s not about what I want now, but for that version of myself—the one who ached for you—to finally have you, if only to see if the happiness he dreamed of was ever real.

I wonder if it would be worth it, to give up everything just to let him feel what he believed you could give. Would it have been enough? Or would the dream of you always be more perfect than the reality? It’s hard to say now, standing here, with the weight of all that’s changed. But still, a part of me wonders…

~anonymous

Just sayin'
Emotion contradicts Intelligence. The more u allow ur emotions to influence, the cloudy ur judgement and action becomes. Emotion is simply a tool developed biologically for surviving, for socializing. But nowadays people give it too damn importance over everything that they start to think like,  being emotional is only human. The main factor that gives us humans superiority over species is not emotion, its cerebral capacity. Instead of wasting time on so-called "normal life achievements (eat,  sleep, shit, get a job, marry, reproduce and die)" we should focus on generational progress, planetary advancements, making dyson reactors, interstellar ships, dimension fabricator, Super A.i etc.
But it's futile to argue with the species who think the heart is capable of making secondary thoughts.

~Nico D Tesla

text:
#1236
It was March 19, and I am still stuck, reflecting on what I studied so hard for but still made mistakes. What a day it was! Ramadan is always my favorite. I remember every part of that day. It rained, and I thought maybe it was a sign of good news coming. I started my "alhamdulliahs" after seeing the rain. I used to love rainy days; they gave me positive energy and filled me with happiness. But I never knew that would be my last rainy day, the one that gave me hope.

The whole day was perfect, but just before Maghrib, a result was published. I didn't find my name on the list. At least I thought I might be on the waiting list? I didn’t cry—just silence. There were a lot of plates and a lot of food arranged on the table, but how could I tell...?

It’s not that I didn’t get a chance anywhere; fun fact, I got accepted everywhere except BUET. I am trying to move on, but every time I do, someone mentions BUET, bringing back all the memories of that day. Why? I got accepted everywhere except there. Why not talk about DU or other places where I was accepted?

How long will I be stuck here? Please, let my varsity life begin. I want to enjoy and forget that day. Someone please tell the KUET authorities that we can’t keep living like this.

Just 3 a.m. thoughts.

~ A depressed soul stuck on March 19

text:
#1226
Lost love. 
The myth. The first, one sided love in school life. The purest kind, when you didn’t even know the full extension of love yet you knew she's the one. Those memories of you trying your hardest to impress her and fate fails to notice u, like her. Yet your love grew strong each day. And then, there she goes, without a warning, without a second to catch, untraceable. The untold kinda love. 
Years have gone yet your heart still beats for her and eyes searching for that one love where love is cheap now. Too malicious and toxic. Impure. The ironically worse part is , those memories you cherished all those years are now fading, leaving you again. Yet you can describe her exactly how she was then. Color of her cheerful eyes, Heavenly smile, beautiful hands that waved at you. 
Sometimes I wonder, would I ever find that love again! Tbh, No. Even if I find her someday, I won't be falling in love with her but the memories. 
Still, it would be nice to hold her hand and say, You are as beautiful since the day i lost you.

~ Alfanzo

Running mindlessly and unfocused, they end up damaging their timeline severely. But for an observer, these mistakes or accidents are a gold mine. Based on them, U can analyze, plan and prepare for the worst, for everything. And when u think and act like that, U end up several steps ahead of your surroundings . U become less ignorant, more of a superior being among beings, surpassing especially on cerebral length.Reality becomes malleable, life becomes easier. When u can imagine, pre-plan and execute properly, u become less dependent and self-sufficient on all sorts of things, especially when it comes to innovative thinking, emotions and socialism. U start to see the world full of,  well, people but slow people.U may regret thinking what could've been achieved if everybody had the same visions. Things may disappoint u a lot. 
And ur friends might call u Narcissist, Manipulator, emotionless robot, born with gifted destiny, Mr perfect, Outcast etc. 
So what? If u are actually better at ideology, thoughts, plans and practical skills than most others , then these words of jealousy are normal and would work like motivations and progress indicators l:v

~ Nico D Tesla '23

text:
#1191
My first year of university life was full of mistakes. Numerous errors, an inferiority complex, and a severe CGPA drop all combined to break me down mentally. I had no idea how I would survive the remaining three years at KUET. It was during this critical time that I met the most important person in my life, who pulled me out of the darkness of my mistake-filled life.

He has always been by my side as my guide, teacher, best friend, advisor, problem solver, and helper. After the CGPA drop in the first year, I never had to look back, thanks to his support from the second year onwards. Today, I have graduated with a respectable CGPA, thanks to his help.

I was someone who constantly felt inferior, but he showed me how to believe in myself and be confident. I used to quietly endure all the injustices against me, but he taught me how to speak up and defend myself. Sometimes, I can’t help but wonder, why didn’t I meet him in my first year? If I had known him back then, maybe I wouldn’t have made those mistakes. I am deeply thankful to him for his invaluable support.

I never say these things to anyone. But today, I felt like sharing them on this platform.

~হামিংবার্ড

text:
#1186
In the virtual world, people often fall in love not just with faces but with words, specifically through chatting. As conversations flow, the person starts feeling special, and their chat ID becomes familiar, bringing joy with every message. Eventually, the desire to hear their voice grows, and phone numbers are exchanged. What started as chatting late into the night turns into long phone calls, and the person no longer feels virtual but close, like someone deeply familiar. However, as time passes, the relationship can unexpectedly fade, and the person who once felt so near becomes a stranger. Only a few manage to hold on to that connection for life, building a future together. Their happiness lights up our news feeds, reminding us of what could have been, as some of us are left with old memories and another sleepless night filled with longing.

~anonymous

text:
#1130
I thought I would never write about this. But suddenly today, I made my words vivid and synchronised and couldn’t resist the urge to write.

Friends. Grouping. Circles. Homies. Dept mates. 
I got dept mates, I admire them. I joke with them. I make fun with them in the class.
But the other words, I don't think I have acquired them. I do have friends, when I don't have any. I can get into a circle easily, but I don't have one of my own. Grouping? I don't have a group to do that. Homies, I am their homies when I meet them,otherwise I am not.
Do they hate me? No.
Do they love me? No.
If I ask them for sudden hangouts or a tea time, they don't say "NO". Maybe they really consider me as a friend. But they never approached me to hang out with them,or to have lunch or dinner. 
I can just take a seat and get involved in any groups gossip session,they don't return me. But they also never offered me to be with them, to have hours of talk with them. 

I am just a guest in those groups,they make me feel wanted only that time when I get to them. Otherwise I am friendless ( maybe sounds selfish). 
How am I surviving then? By being lonely among peoples, they don't hate me,they don't love me. 
Real life NPC. 

Life isn't that bad actually,but life can be harsh sometimes. 
Cheers to those people for accepting me whenever I run to them!

~anonymous

Our life is like a K-drama if our partner is  green forest. It's a blessing that I have found someone like him. He looks at me without me realizing it. He makes a lot of sacrifices for me without me knowing. But those things aren't evident until we see or learn about them in some way. Once, he rode 15 kilometers on his bicycle just to catch a glimpse of me. The day we entered into a relationship, he makes that day special every year. Every year on that day, he changes his profile picture, and he doesn't do that at any other time. We don’t feel like celebrating that day outwardly, but in our own way, we acknowledge that we remember it. We are in a long-distance relationship and it's secret. Yet, he drops everything and comes to me when I want to meet him. We have never made our relationship public and there’s a kind of peace in that. It helps avoid toxicity. If there’s ever a problem with him or me, we have no other option but to talk to each other. And this brings about better solutions. It keeps our understanding strong. Our life is like a K-drama. The only thing that increases toxicity is not being able to be completely open with each other.

~anonymous('23)

#1121
My life had been miserable for the past few days. I had lost interest in everything, and I spent every night sleepless, filled with despair. Then, one day, I saw a beautiful girl near pocket gate. I didn’t know her name or which department she belonged to, but every time I saw her, all of my pain and sorrows seemed to vanish. It was as if she brought color back into my life. My once-joyful spirit returned, and all my problems faded away. 

She unknowingly gave me a new sense of purpose, lifting me from the depths of my misery. A man who had been lost in depression suddenly found happiness, all because of her. After a week of silently admiring her, I discovered that she belongs to the CSE department. 

I just want to thank her for saving me from that dark, miserable period of my life. Even though she may never know the impact she had on me, I will always be grateful for the light she brought into my world.

~someone from 2K22

#1114
Introduction:
The department’s self-proclaimed experts are analyzing how rainy weather influences our collective decision to skip class and binge nap instead.

Objectives:

1. To quantify how much rain is needed to trigger a full-on class avoidance mode.

2. To determine the exact point where the weather makes class attendance feel like an optional extra.

Apparatus Required:

Weather forecasts (for optimal nap timing)

Attendance records (to track how many of us are MIA)

Student surveys (to measure how badly we’d rather be asleep)

Calculator (to confirm that rain + class = zero motivation)

Calculation:

1. Cross-reference class attendance with weather conditions to measure impact.

2. Analyze survey responses on how much rain increases our desire to skip out and sleep in.

3. Calculate the correlation between rainy days and our rapidly declining interest in attending lectures.

Discussion:
The data is clear: rain = immediate drop in class attendance and a dramatic spike in nap time. Our “research” shows that even a light drizzle can turn a 9 AM lecture into a glorified pillow party.

Conclusion:
Rain massively boosts the likelihood of ditching class for a nap. This study confirms that our commitment to napping far exceeds our commitment to attending lectures when the weather turns gloomy.

~anonymous(EEE'21)

#1100
Have you ever found yourself wide awake while the bustling city takes a break, pondering the mysteries of life?
When the once chaotic streets plunge into silence, you surreptitiously go through the old photographs. As you scroll one after another, you can feel your heart pacing, vision getting blurry, yet the photographs remain vividly etched in your memory. You slowly find yourself stuck in an endless loop, replaying every glimpse, every whispered word, every smile. At times it gets exhausting, right? Though the forbidden realm feels ethereal.
Perhaps it’s the caffeine, or perhaps it’s just my mind refusing to find tranquility.

~limerant (ME'23)

text:
#1071
My whole existence feels so heavy these days. Every day I wake up and a sudden wave of pain paralyzes me. Like an old memory, it suddenly hits me and blocks my mind for the day. I don't know how to exactly describe this thing. But, I feel like I am drowning in it. Day by day, I am loosing myself and control over my actions. My head feels so cluttered that I am struggling to gather my thoughts. 

Though it's not all bad. It cured my overthinking a little bit. When you can't even think straight, you can't overthink. It's a win I guess.

I am not sad or depressed about it actually. It's like, the pain is so soul consumig that you can't even think about it. The sufferings overwhelms the nuerons so much that you go into a trance. 

It was like this. the brain could no longer bear the worries and pains that were imposed on it. it said: "I'm giving up; but if there is anyone else here who is interested in preserving the whole, let him assume part of my burden and it will be alright for a bit."
Franz Kafka, Letters to Milena

I don't know for how long I can keep myself afloat before I drown in it for good. I just hope I get my peace down there. I hope I get myself by loosing me.

~anonymous

text:
#1063
This isn't something usually tied to this place, but a random thought crossed my mind, and I felt like sharing it. How do you guys deal when you develop feelings for someone who doesn't feel the same way? Maybe they once did, but one incident completely changed their natural feelings, molding them into someone different. It's like watching an actor or actress play two completely different roles in separate movies. The hardest part is that you can't get over it, not in days or weeks, but sometimes for months—because of the bond you had, to be honest. You've been dealing with it for years, and no matter how much you've tried to explain how hard it's been, they just don't understand. Their feelings have shifted, becoming a different version of what they once were.

They want you to move on. They even pity your feelings and may check in occasionally to make sure you're okay—but that just makes it worse because you can't accept the reality that they're not meant to be in your life anymore.

People call it a "skill issue"—you hear the advice: "Come on, there are so many people in the world. Surely there's someone who matches your vibe. If you can't find them, it's your fault." But after investing 3, 4, even 5 years into this person, when you're obsessed with every little thing about how they once cared for you, it's hard to accept that. She wasn't the mistake—you were, for rushing into the bond without thinking about the risks. Now, you're stuck. You're not like others, who can move on with ease, and after all these years, she has finally left.

Every day, when you wake up, you're trying to think about normal things—school, work, family—but suddenly, she pops into your mind, making you lie down just a little longer. You ask yourself why you fell into these feelings when you knew it could lead to this never-ending hole.

As you look around, it feels like people are robots. They deal with heartbreak like it's just another part of life, always in the "show must go on" mode. You wonder why you can't be like them, why you can't let it go as easily as they did. You start feeling like you're making one issue into 100, and it can't be that big, can it?

People tell you to "get busy, and you won't have time to think about it." But even when you're busy, there are moments when you're with a group of friends, everyone's laughing, and suddenly, you feel that loneliness creep in. You can't talk about it with them—they'll just tell you, "Come on man, deal with it. That one person doesn't define your life. They left because God has a better plan for you. Stop hurting yourself." As if you haven't been trying to stop the pain.

You never had that kind of close friend, a "painkilling diary" of sorts, who could listen to everything you're going through, and who would open up to you the same way. The bond you had was unique, but it was more platonic on her side, and you realized that too late. Confessing your feelings after all that time led to the separation of both of you in the end.

Years passed in the blink of an eye. She's moving on, not dwelling on why your feelings had to land on her, while you're still complaining about life at every step. People call it a "skill issue," and that just brings you down even further because, even if she wasn't "the one," you still wanted her around so you wouldn't feel like you'd lost your grip on life and fallen into a dark hole.

It's like you're a level 1 noob in realism, living in a video game where everything around you feels like an Unreal Engine simulation.

You eventually feel so low that it doesn't feel useful to tell others, because they haven't experienced what it's like to lose someone who was once there for all your highs and lows. And now, because of one mistake—expressing how you really felt—you've destroyed whatever togetherness there was.

Now you don't have that special shield to protect you when you feel down, and your mental state is getting worse. But, like the song "Tears" by HEALTH says, "You have to let it go. It's been long enough." So, you try. You move on. You fail. Then you try again. Because life won't stop for you, right?

~anonymous('22)

Before I could explain, she expressed feeling overwhelmed within seconds. At that moment, I felt as if I had emotionally cheated on her. I confessed that I hadn't written the letter, and I felt immense guilt for not having written something similar to her myself. She was embarrassed when I revealed that it wasn't my writing. I assure you that I had no intention of pretending or deceiving her..

She popped out of nowhere and she is now my everything. 

~anonymous

#1033
Okay, so about my last writing, #1000, it seemed like you all liked it a lot. Here’s another one, for those who are feeling disappointed, depressed, or hopeless—or maybe just for anyone who is not at their best.

One day, you'll forget the sadness you felt when you saw the undeserving result on the notice board. Every sleepless night of study before the semester finals will pay off, even if not in the way you expected.

Eventually, the helplessness you felt when you were alone in a crowd will fade away. You will emerge stronger and more resilient than ever. 

Soon, the scolding you received from your teacher for not completing your lab correctly will be forgotten, and you will reap the rewards of the 2.5 hours of hard work you put in each day.

Before long, your shyness and anxiety during presentations will vanish, replaced by confidence, logic, and a touch of humor, embodied by a smart, strong individual who stands tall.

One day, you will forget the bitterness and betrayal from friends, only to cherish the sweet and welcoming moments you shared with them.

In time, the fear of being average that you felt throughout your university life will be replaced by tears of joy from your mother when she sees you succeed.

Soon, those who once tried to bring you down will become the ones who see you as their hero. You’ll look back on your struggles with a cheeky smile.

Ultimately  Your life will align just like that of the friend you always admired. You’ll realize that it wasn’t your time then that kept you from shining like your renowned friend.

Finally, you’ll see that all the regrets about not becoming the person you wanted to be, not achieving the results you desired, not getting the person you loved, or not having life turn out as you hoped were all inconsequential. 
You’ll come to appreciate the life destined for you by God and recognize it as the best life you could ever lead.

Everything will be alright. Life will go on. 
Take care.

~anonymous

One day, you'll forget the sadness you felt when you saw the undeserving result on the notice board. Every sleepless night of study before the semester finals will pay off, even if not in the way you expected.  Eventually, the helplessness you felt when you were alone in a crowd will fade away. You will emerge stronger and more resilient than ever.   Soon, the scolding you received from your teacher for not completing your lab correctly will be forgotten, and you will reap the rewards of the 2.5 hours of hard work you put in each day.  Before long, your shyness and anxiety during presentations will vanish, replaced by confidence, logic, and a touch of humor, embodied by a smart, strong individual who stands tall.  One day, you will forget the bitterness and betrayal from friends, only to cherish the sweet and welcoming moments you shared with them.  In time, the fear of being average that you felt throughout your university life will be replaced by tears of joy from your mother when she sees you succeed.  Soon, those who once tried to bring you down will become the ones who see you as their hero. You’ll look back on your struggles with a cheeky smile.  Ultimately  Your life will align just like that of the friend you always admired. You’ll realize that it wasn’t your time then that kept you from shining like your renowned friend.  Finally, you’ll see that all the regrets about not becoming the person you wanted to be, not achieving the results you desired, not getting the person you loved, or not having life turn out as you hoped were all inconsequential.  You’ll come to appreciate the life destined for you by God and recognize it as the best life you could ever lead.  Everything will be alright. Life will go on.  Take care.

text:
#1029
Dear Alif Lam Mim,

Do you remember my first impression after seeing your name on Boo was this? I wrote, "Alif Lam Mim ________ apnar nam ta shundor."  And then you thanked me. Probably, you don’t. It’s not that important enough to remember. I don’t know if you’ll ever see this or not, but I still hope you do. You know, I wrote a lot of letters to you when we weren’t talking. It’s hard to resist the urge to text or call you. So the closest alternative I found is writing letters that will never reach you.

Surprisingly, now it's all about you. When I wake up, I think of you. When I listen to music, I think of you. When I'm out with friends, I think of you. Everything I do reminds me of you..Whenever I go on YouTube, I see your suggested songs.My whole playlist is filled with your suggestions. I can’t even do anything peacefully because you keep popping into my mind. 
I see you everywhere , in everything. Even a single notification with your name works like magic on me—it makes my day. You’re like ""kalo jeera"" to me. (Kalo jeera is believed to be the remedy for all diseases.)

The saddest thing is that your emotion (I don’t know how to put it in words..I don't know any other suitable words for what we had with each other) was there briefly,  but mine wasn't. I fell for you harder and hopelessly. Now, I don’t know what else to do anymore. I really miss you , crazily.  It feels like there’s an empty void in me, like a piece of me is missing. I don’t know how or when I became this attached to you, but a day without talking to you doesn't feel like a day at all. I wish I were from somewhere you are. 
It hurts to see that my biggest fear came true. You can’t even imagine how much it hurts. I hope you never have to face this endless loneliness. I hope you never have to wander in the loop of hopelessness..

Sometimes, I think I should have been like you—not this extremely monogamous, unable to even think of anyone else but you. I really should have been a perfectly functional human being like you, caring less for others (not others just me )...

But anyways, thank you so much for the time you were with me. Be happy always, as you were every time. Take care, dehydrated human. Remember to drink water.

~JJ

I can relate dear, stranger. As a man, I've accepted that validation is something that is not necessary. It's certainly a plus but not really mandatory.
Once you leave your small universe at your home, the vastness of the world will take you by surprise!
You will know that the only person standing by your side is you and nobody else. Always try to help yourself. Forgive yourself. Appreciate yourself. Validate yourself. Hold onto your thoughts, and keep improving them. Build bonds, but don't indulge into them so much that you start drowning in them.
Life is cruel to some of us. Never give in to this. Don't give up without putting up a fight. Hope your journey in KUET will be a bright chapter in your life.

text:
#1024
Is love truly a basic human need? If so, why do I lack the desire for it? At almost 25, I've never felt drawn to anyone. While others around me are falling in love, getting engaged, and tying the knot, I find myself content in solitude. I seem to have no capacity for loving anyone. Perhaps, instead, I have an enduring dislike for others. I wonder if I am the most selfish and self-centered person. Why am I so distant? Soon, I'll be leaving this campus with nothing but an empty heart and soul.

~anonymous

text:
#1013
I hope you’re doing okay. I’ve been thinking a lot about everything we’ve been through. It’s hard to believe we’ve ended up here, and I can’t stop missing you. The good times we had together are still fresh in my mind, and it hurts to think that we’re apart now.
I know we had our differences, and I’m sorry if I made you feel like I didn’t care enough. I really did care about you, even if it didn’t always show. I wish we could have understood each other better, and maybe things would have been different.
Even though I miss you, I also remember how I felt sometimes—that I wasn’t valued as much as I needed to be. That’s something that’s hard for me to forget. I know we both made mistakes, and maybe there are things we could have done differently, but now all I can do is try to move forward and heal.
Please know that I will always remember the good times we shared. I hope we can both find peace and learn from this experience, even if we’re no longer together.
I wish you the best and hope you find what you’re looking for too. TAKE CARE

~anonymous

text:
#1006
Dear nothing,
Today everyone is sending letters to someone they love or care for, someone who is important. So I am writing this to you. You will probably never even read it. I am writing to you because you are important to me. You are my favourite nothing. So many days have passed, and I still can't figure out what you are to me, what to call you, and how to address you. I decided I would forget you like you never even existed like I never even noticed you. But the heart doesn't ever listen. One thing I have realized is that no matter how many people say love is all that matters is not true. What matters is having a relationship where you are acknowledged; when you die, those people will be remembered to be a part of your life with whom you have a relation like your wife or gf or your famous lover,whom you have given a name.I won't matter,my love for you won't matter.Those who are nothing never matters.But still when I wake from sleep,you cross my mind.I see you posted something and my heart aches a little bit.But I will end all of these.It is embarrassing,you know.I can't see myself like this.I want this day to come sooner when your name pops up and nothing happens.I want to feel nothing for you.I will emit you totally from my life.I was listening to this song "tum ko" when I was going literally mad for you.I will not listen to this song now.But few months later,when I listen to this song again,I should not remember you at all and then everything will be back to normal.Though I have this little wish in my heart that you will at least be something someday but I will give up this hope.This is not good for me and I have promised to always be good to myself no matter what.You live far away from me,I wish you the best for your life.I will also be back to my normal self which doesn't care about you at all .I don't know how to end this letter which will never reach you.But I have loved you but "tomar dewa amar kono naam chilona" tai ei love o r matter korlona.

~anonymous

text:
#1000
I am currently in the final phase of my time at KUET, and I see many hopeless romantics around. So, I want to share the journey of my unrequited love for a classmate. Perhaps this will help ease the pain and suffering of those who are struggling.

One day, you will stop rereading the few small conversations you had with her. You’ll move on from those moments that once felt so significant.

Eventually, you will stop going into her inbox, only to leave without texting her, realizing that your messages have little to no impact on her life.

In time, you will stop asking the stars to make her yours, and you’ll stop saying things like, “The moon is beautiful, isn’t it?” The desire to reach out to her will start to fade.

Before long, the helplessness you feel today—the pain of not having her—will disappear. Your constant efforts to make her notice you will lessen.

Soon, the sadness you feel when you see a post of hers, with eyes filled with tears, will be gone. Eventually, you will realize it’s not about your shortcomings but rather fate that has kept you from being with her.

In time, you will stop asking her to create memories with you that you can cherish forever. The longing for such moments will diminish.

Soon, you will feel happy that although you may not have won her over, you discovered yourself through the process. You fought hard to become more worthy, and eventually, you became a better person.

Before long, you will be free from the intense feelings you once had—the pressure and racing heart whenever you tried to get close to her.

Ultimately, you will give up and eventually feel nothing when you see her. You will finally say goodbye and let her go. All that will remain is a wish for her to find someone who will love her just as you did. In the end, everything will be alright, and life will go on.

Take care.

~anonymous

text:
#995
Through conversations with various people, I've noticed a pattern: many men seem to believe that boys and girls can't be just friends, suggesting that there will always be something more—some underlying attraction or tension—between them. This perspective might align with the idea that our nature, possibly shaped by biological or social factors, makes purely platonic relationships between men and women less likely.
On the other hand, many women view the situation differently. They often consider it perfectly normal to have friendships with men, finding it entirely feasible to hang out, enjoy each other's company, and maintain a purely platonic relationship without any romantic undertones.(If you don't agree with my observation, please feel free to share your thoughts in the comments.)
This difference in perspectives raises an intriguing question: Why isn't there a more balanced view? Why do many women seem comfortable with the idea of male-female friendships, while many men do not? Can we explore this topic in a general sense without pointing to specific cases or examples?
I want to emphasize that my intention isn't to hurt anyone's feelings or stir up controversy. I'm simply curious to understand the underlying thoughts and beliefs behind these differing viewpoints.

~anonymous

text:
#977

To
Riasat Alam
ECE 18

So, there's this guy... I've been crushing on him since last year. I first saw him hanging out with his friends, smoking and hillariously laughing about something. And I was instantly drawn to his energy. Then came "University Day", his band started off with a song that was really close to my heart, "Yellow" by Coldplay. Even though he wasn't singing the song, I couldn't take my eyes off of him. Such was his aura and stage presence. Then he played a Metallica song that I still don't know the name of. The way he played and sang the song, it felt like he owned the whole auditorium.

I started stalking him online and offline, and I couldn't find a single bad thing about him. I was totally smitten. Then came "Onuronon", I saw him rap which seemed like a folk song at first. And I was blown away because "Whoa! He can rap too!?" And the way he swayed the crowd with his guitar solo of Numb, I've never seen anyone command a crowd so effortlessly with just a guitar. So captivating, it made my heart race.

I'd go to every event on campus just to see him. Watching him perform at the "Acoustic Night" and the rag concert was so bittersweet, knowing it might be my last chance seeing him in KUET. When he sang "I Wanna Be Yours," I couldn't help but imagine him singing it to me.

I have never felt so strongly about a mere "guy". None of my friends know about my feelings for him. I don't think I could ever tell him how I feel. But I hope he continues to shine bright, just like he did on the stages.

~Someone from his junior batches.

Rather he started saying, "ইঞ্জিনিয়াররর...  কিন্তু কিচ্ছু জানেনা। সামনে দেখবো, ইঞ্জিনিয়ার হয়ে কি করতে পারিস. <Engineer this, engineer that>" This breaks my spirit. He behaves like this on baseless things. What is going to happen after graduation? Allah knows. I heard, daughters are the apple of eyes of their dads. What can I do to become the apple of his eyes? This is just a very small issue I stated out of many issues.   Things are way more complex, I am stuck in that quicksand, tried to find the way out. But no solution is effective. I am just passing the days like this!

text:
#962
I didn’t take any notice of my boiling heart that kept itself hidden since the day I saw you in October first. I kept glancing at you and before long I had developed a habit of keeping you in my eyesight during the entirety of the classes. And it only slipped away more as we faced each other in the lab. Then long breaks came, for month I have been away from Telighati and it seemed like a fucking blessing, to be away from that shithole. Then, as restlessness swept over me I more and more thought of returning. Why am I missing Telighati out of all the places? I guess my heart was scorched by then because I smelt a burn. Everyone knows when your breath smells like fire, you are in love. And I knew it was you because out of all the people in my class I looked at you the most l, so much so that it became a habit. Did you see the flames in my eyes that I could not contain when you ironically asked me what do I think if you marry early? 

I wrote poems for you that only you read and with a concealed blow you said you don’t have a liking for poetry or literature in general. It’s sad to see lovers growing an appetite for cruelty as if pain is the cost to be paid so that we are cherished and adored. Growing up we thought of these as granted. And now we don’t know how to achieve these. 

You are tall, beautiful. You hide your eyes behind glasses to stop the Medusa-gaze. You have a lushness to your skin that only flowers have. But what I love most about you is your fingers. Your long fingers are so thin that one might think you have no muscle on them. But no one finds them sickly or unusual. You graciously stroke them over the keyboard and I stare in the lab. Once I was accused of writing a confession to one of my classmates. I never wrote it and I said to them what’s the point if she doesn’t even know who feels so extremely about her. And this isn’t a confession. It's something I can’t tell you because I’m so blinded by fantasy that I can’t really assess how you feel about me so there is no point. And it’s something I can’t call a poem because the blow has steered away the metaphors and eulogies. What I’m left with is the dryness of inability to either bloom or perish. 

~anonymous

Correct me if I am wrong but I believe, a girl can sense from a mile away whether someone, who's been talking with her for a while, is going to ask her out soon.

As a hopeless romantic I have encountered with both type of girls, one who just want you to confess so that she can reject you and add another trophy to her "list" and announce it publicly as if it's some kind of achievement, another who just want to get rid of you as soon as she's sensed that you like her. 

I just want to pay my deepest respect to that girl who ignored me, gave me obvious signs that she won't accept me  had I asked her out. You could have acted like you had no idea what's going on and could have made me ask you out. You too could have go around and tell the whole kuet  that I had asked you out. You too could have had some 'fun' with your roommates  by giving obscure signals so that I keep chasing you while you laugh & eat chips with your roomies.  

But you were direct & clear. You didn't do any of that. And that's why I have so much respect for you. I pray and hope that you get the man of your dreams and I hope all of your wishes come true! Thank you, Thank you so much. I bet the boy who wins you over is going to be so lucky!

Also my heartfelt gratitude  to all the girls, who were direct and clear,  even when you could have had made a boy go crazy by giving subtle signals or a obscure answer. You didn't care about having some cheap enjoyment on the expense of a man's sanity. Thank you for that. Remember, that is your class.  If I ever have a daughter,  I would try my best to raise her like your parents did.

~anonymous

text:
#944
Suddenly, I am feeling so hopeless, like I am losing myself, my entity. 
Once upon a time, I had a lot of dreams. I dreamt of being a good person, helping others, buying a Tesla, working at Space-X, inventing things for the betterment of mankind...
 I know it was quite childish but rather I could even imagine myself achieving those dreams. I was full of inspiration and desire to be great and noble.
But day by day, all of my dreams are fading away. I am no longer a lively person. Now, my only goal is to achieve good marks in class tests and term finals. Sometimes, I don't even know what I am learning or where I'm heading. I feel lost, unsure of the purpose of my life. 
The more I grow older, the less I can dream. Days are just passing by, and the output of my life seems to be zero. I don't even know what I will do after graduation. I can't dream anymore. I feel like I'm good for nothing—just worthless.

~anonymous

#936

Writing this thing here may be inappropriate but this is my last message in KUET whispers. 
Love doesn’t mean a relationship, right? I love someone but I don’t feel like being with him. He also made it very clear that he doesn’t love me at all still I hope. It’s my fault I agree. His intelligence, beauty, and status are way outta my league.  I thought we would always stay together.  Sometimes I wonder what I could do to be a match for him. When I see other girls I realise why I can't be with him. They seem to be perfect.  He is so perfect indeed. I am not asking you to be with me. Can't I just wait for you while silently being in love with you? I just want my parents not to marry me to another guy. I wish to love him forever even when he is with his partner happily. Is it too much to ask for? I don’t anything from him even. He will be leaving soon. I won’t get to see him or talk to him maybe. I will be fine. And it doesn’t make me sad anymore. I learned to let him go. Now when my friends tease me saying, "Khub to prem korteso." I don’t argue anymore. Cause deep down, I know what I lose.
I pray for your happiness dear and be with someone you love. I will be always yours In Sha Allah. 
Thank you and sorry to those who read this useless thing of mine. Allah Hafez 

~anonymous

I was in a relationship with someone 2 years ago.  I didn't hesitate to give my all for his happiness. He promised never to leave me. I also believed like a fool. Everything was going well. But I always had the fear of losing him. So I decided to marry him quietly. And when his graduation is complete, we will inform the family and get married again. He agreed.  But in the meantime, he suddenly reduced the conversation. Then suddenly one day he started saying that his mother would not accept me in any way. Because I am not a girl from their area. And he can't marry me against his parents' wishes. Besides, they will never accept the fact that my parents are divorced.  Finally, he decided to leave me. I tried hard but couldn't stop it. I'm so mentally broken I don't even have the last will to live. I know what I did was wrong. I'm getting punished for it.  I don't have the courage to tell my parents what happened to me. If my parents get to know about that they may never forgive me. I don't know what will happen to me. I will never be able to forget what happened. I don't have the courage to commit suicide. But it's hard to live with this. I don't know what to do. I hate myself the most. I gave everything to such a wrong man. He promised to marry me. Then he never felt the need to know his parents' wishes. But today he tells me he can't continue the relationship against his parents' wishes.

I'm posting about a situation involving my non-KUETian friend. I didn't have a reliable place to seek advice, so I'm sharing it here.

In 2019, my friend was r/ped. She didn't tell me at the time, and I just found out recently. She asked me not to share this information with anyone. Her ra*pist who was a classmate from a different department, threatened to ruin her life if she spoke up.

She doesn't want her parents to know and hasn’t fought back; she has suffered in silence. I know the rapist’s name, but I don't know what to do. Even though it’s been years, I can't just ignore this. I understand that I don't have proof and my friend wants to keep it quiet because she's still afraid of the attacker.

I’m concerned that the ra*pist is living freely without consequences. He is leading a normal life whereas my friend is still traumatised. It ruined her mental health. I wonder how many others like him are out there without facing any punishment. What should I do?

-anonymous

text:
#882
Really speechless after reading post 860.

Firstly, I want to address something to the people commenting. Some people are suggesting, "Tell your Mum," "Raise your voice," and so on. Things are not that simple. When a woman is raped or sexually assaulted, people often question her attire or whether she was alone with a "protective" male companion. In this case, these factors don't apply. Why? Because the perpetrator is the FATHER, the bloody father, perhaps the sole breadwinner of the family. A typical mother will do almost anything to protect her partner's image, because it's a matter of family reputation, and she won't risk damaging his so-called fragile masculine ego. Now is not the time to discuss this problem openly. Assuming the girl is only 20 years old, she is still financially dependent on her family.

Secondly, I have something to say to that girl. First, a big hug to you for being brave enough to fight for yourself and strong for your mother and your other biological parent. I am incredibly proud of you for finding the courage to share your story with us, raising awareness about the unspoken fears and abuse inflicted by men since the beginning of time. I hope you can find a small group of people to trust and rely on while remaining vigilant.

When I first read the post, a rush of overwhelming feelings swept over me. I was reminded of the Josef Fritzl case in Germany. The question of how and why this happened isn't important. We should care more about the girl who went through this terrible thing and how awful it must have been for her to keep it a secret.

Finally, I want to express my gratitude to the people who prayed for her, sympathized with her, and showed her empathy.

To those saying wtf, wth, egula ki, eta fake- dude grow some empathy within yourself. Tomar cheye chatgpt o bhalo empathy dekhay. Imagine the immense pain she must have endured.

LASTLY,
No matter what storms life brings your way, I hope you rise above them with strength and find your way to a brighter future.

-anonymous

text:
#877
Since everyone’s opening up about their horrible experiences, I should share mine too. It’s not something I usually do, but reading through all the comments made me feel something I haven’t felt in a long time, relief, even if just for a few moments. It’s strange how comforting it can be to see that you’re not alone in this darkness and that others understand this constant, crushing pain. For us, those moments of relief are so rare, yet here I am, feeling a little less alone. 

So, I was 8 years old when my cousin who was a College student started to rape me. It went on for almost a year. I didn’t even know what was happening to me, I was so traumatized, so lost in the confusion and fear. I told my parents, hoping they would make it stop, but nothing changed. No action was taken against him. Instead, he kept coming around, continuing his abuse as if nothing was wrong. 

After a year of living in constant fear, I became so terrified of him that I started to avoid him at all costs. I wouldn’t go near him, no matter what. That’s how it finally stopped. But it didn’t really end, did it? He still comes to our house, sits at our table, and eats with us. And every time he sees me, he smiles. It’s a smile that only I understand, a twisted reminder of what he’s done. Every time I see that smile, the trauma hits me all over again, like a wound that never really heals. I got suicidal and made multiple attempts but failed to do so because I was a coward. 

Then one day, I finally left home. I chose KUET, even after getting into DU and BUP. I still chose KUET, and I had to fight so hard for it. I left home not because I wanted to, but because I had to, just to avoid him, to escape family gatherings where I’d inevitably run into him. Now, my home is Rokeya Hall. 

I envy people who say KUET is hell for them because that means they have healthy families to go back to. But for me, KUET is heaven. It’s the one place where I don’t have to face him, where I don’t have to live in constant fear. After being traumatized for almost 20 years of my life, I finally found a place where I could breathe, and where I wasn’t scared every second of the day. But now, with everything that’s been happening, I don’t even feel safe here anymore. 

Some of my batchmates’ attitudes towards girls have only added to my trauma. I’m terrified, and I feel like I have nowhere left to hide. I’m begging, actually begging, every guy on campus, can you please provide a safe and healthy environment for girls like me to live without fear? I’m planning to leave the country once I graduate and become financially independent. But until then, I have nowhere else to go. I know I sound desperate, but I am. I’m really scared to go back to campus after everything that’s happened. I’m literally bursting into tears as I write this. Please forgive me if I’ve said anything wrong, my mind is a mess right now. Sorry for the long post, but I just needed to get this out.

-anonymous

text:
#876
The idea behind KUET Whispers was good, in some senses great. I have been following this page for a long time - sometimes getting goosebumps, feeling others' pain,  smiles, and laughter relating. I acknowledge it has been an awesome experience. Thank you for that.

The motto 'Whisper your deepest secrets' certainly defines this page's character the best. Kudos to the admins for maintaining these characteristics for so long. 

Completely anonymous posting has its own advantages and disadvantages, well regulation has suppressed most of the disadvantages till now. Yes, the Indian incident has shaken us all, we all deeply condemn and mourn. This horrific event's surge has suddenly made us vocal against all kinds of oppression and harassment, and we should be always.

But being a page allowing anonymous posting - some responsibility boils down to you. The are limits to set. Post-accusing someone from an anonymous end - can be exploitative. For example: Someone posts about someone's intention, that he/she has assumed. Should it be judged taking one party's view? Imagine if that incident never happened / the Accused person didn't notice/was in their subconscious mind. How can the accused person defend oneself? Defaming someone in a "চালায় দেন" manner. Take the above one as an example it does not have any relation with what I'll be writing now.

Sadik Sir is a very introverted person as I know him. He talks very often in any meetup. After these two whispers posted related to him, I have been keeping communication with him. I have asked him to clear his position - but he is unable to decide what to post. I don't want to go into any specifics, I wish Allah would give him the courage to speak up and make it easy for him.

But I want to mention one thing, Though the first post claims - the second post is based on assumption. 

I know, your intentions are pure, but please be careful with the limitations and challenges of anonymous posting. Please do not let anyone weaponize this page for defaming /insulting.

text:
#875
There’s been a lot of talk, but not much substance—allegations without proof, and no names for either the victims or the accused. While it’s important to raise concerns, unverified claims alone won’t lead to real action. Anyone could post a story online, pointing fingers without evidence, and it wouldn’t be possible to know what’s true. Simply sharing your story anonymously, without identifying those involved, won’t get you the justice you’re seeking.
If you’re serious about making a difference, I urge you to reveal both your identity and the identity of the accused. Present any evidence you have, even if it’s not conclusive—naming the individual is a necessary first step. This will allow for a proper investigation to determine the truth. Justice requires transparency and accountability, and I stand with those committed to finding it.

If your goal is simply to have your story heard, then by all means, continue. But understand that without taking further steps, this won’t bring you the justice you deserve.

-anonymous

text:
#867
I was in class 3 or 4 maybe, and a very distant relative of mine touched my skirt and tried to put his hands under it. Everyone else was sitting at a distance watching television, no one noticed. I somehow got up and left the room as fast as I could. 

I was in class 6, and my brother-in-law ( from a cousin ) grabbed my breast and said to my ear " ekdom chup " while his wife was in the kitchen.

A childhood friend from class 1-2 ( later got disconnected for a long time ) touched my thigh when I was in class 8 or 9 . and removed it as soon as I gave him a look.

It's been almost 12 years since the first incident. I don’t think of them that much but the recent posts from everyone brought back all the memories. For the longest time, I thought they didn’t affect me much, tried to be a tough kid but later realized our subconscious mind remembers it all. Maybe they are the very reason why I do not remember anything ( almost ) from my childhood.

As I grew older I developed a thick skin unlike many girls, maybe that's why nothing similar to these have happened after that time. I am no longer a vulnerable little girl a guy can pass a comment to, I act tough so guys can't take a chance. But girls can always feel the eyes on them without even looking.

I am fortunate enough to have a few very good male friends and I still believe 'not all men'. But somehow it's always a man, no? I thought of carrying these secrets to my grave,  so please do not suggest complaining to the police or sharing them with my parents. I am praying to the Almighty for my justice, and I hope some of you will pray for it too. I believe in his plans and I hope for a better tomorrow for all of us.

- One of you.

A childhood friend from class 1-2 ( later got disconnected for a long time ) touched my thigh when I was in class 8 or 9 . and removed it as soon as I gave him a look.  It's been almost 12 years since the first incident. I don’t think of them that much but the recent posts from everyone brought back all the memories. For the longest time, I thought they didn’t affect me much, tried to be a tough kid but later realized our subconscious mind remembers it all. Maybe they are the very reason why I do not remember anything ( almost ) from my childhood.  As I grew older I developed a thick skin unlike many girls, maybe that's why nothing similar to these have happened after that time. I am no longer a vulnerable little girl a guy can pass a comment to, I act tough so guys can't take a chance. But girls can always feel the eyes on them without even looking.  I am fortunate enough to have a few very good male friends and I still believe 'not all men'. But somehow it's always a man, no? I thought of carrying these secrets to my grave,  so please do not suggest complaining to the police or sharing them with my parents. I am praying to the Almighty for my justice, and I hope some of you will pray for it too. I believe in his plans and I hope for a better tomorrow for all of us.  - One of you.

text:
#864
KUET will never be the same for me again. I always knew that predators disguised as men existed everywhere, but to survive, I had convinced myself, heart and soul, that KUET was different, that it was a safe place. Even when I saw things that troubled me, I chose to stay blind and deaf. I convinced myself that his intention was pure, maybe it was just a mistake, maybe he wasn’t checking out my breasts, maybe he didn’t mean to.. maybe I was overthinking. But I can't keep lying to myself anymore. I can never again walk through campus, and classes like a free bird, feeling safe and unbothered. I used to go out for walks when I felt down, wandering around the campus without caring about the world. When my loved ones worried, I’d reassure them, saying, "Pera nish na, campus ei achi, safe achi, ekhane ki e ba hobe!" How naive and blind I was. Now, the very thought of stepping outside fills me with a dread I can’t shake.

-anonymous

maybe it’s not a major story like others but it is major for me. 

During 1-1 days we make lots of friends. We don’t judge according to background. We make non-departmental friends as well. I did the same. It's a common scenario that boys hide their relationship status during the first year. Even if they have a girlfriend they don’t wanna claim it to look cool or to impress idk what. I still don’t know what was my fault in the first place.

text:
#849
To all the girls being vocal about their bad experiences, more power to you. It takes a lot to face your monsters and talk about them.

But, let me share a story. A few months ago, one of our juniors faced something like that. Eventually, whole department knew about it but decided to take things lightly. Some us wanted to help her to take necessary steps. We were adamant that the perpetrator should be punished. I talked with one of my female batchmates and asked her to let her know that we will do everything necessary to support her. But she(junior) decided to go silent about it in the end.

Now, I can't imagine how much pressure a victim goes through and I respect her decision. What pains me is, that guy is still roaming free and will continue his act.

So, it's my humble request to all the girls, do expose them. Maybe start a #metoo movement whenever you are ready. You will find us standing with you.

~anonymous

But I know very well if he would know, he would break this friendship from the beginning. I found him, I lost him again found him and again lost him . And it's like a chain process now. Right now I'm trying to let him go. But I don't think I can. Bro I cried for him in front of my Allah. How can I let him go so easily? "The art of letting go" - Anonymous

text:
#790
First, I want to thank all the people who have taken the time to give me these suggestions. I don’t know what I have done to deserve such amazing seniors. I literally cried for half an hour reading your comments.
To Urmi Apu, I wish to visit your room one day. I don't know if I will ever have the courage to share all these things going on inside me, but I can't explain how comforting I found your comments. Thank you so much.

Thank you, Tahmid Bhaiya. All your suggestions are really helpful. Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a long and helpful comment. Thank you so, so much.

Thanks to the person who defended me in that certain comment. Maybe the person who made the comment didn’t mean anything negative. But as you said, the world revolves differently for each of us. As I didn’t know what he meant to say, I started questioning myself, do I really have the right to feel this way when all these people are suffering around me? I regretted posting. Thanks for letting me know I am not wrong. I have lost count after how many months I have spent my night without feeling the pain in my chest. All thanks to your support. I am really grateful. Sometimes we don't realize how little it takes to make you feel shitty, how little it takes to make you feel whole. I wish there were more sensible souls like you around us. Thank you for existing. Stay blessed.

Thanks to everyone who provided me the information I asked for. Each of you made me realize that I can reach out to any of you whenever I want to. Thank you for offering me help so nicely. I personally don't know any of you still I am really grateful to have you around me.

Special thanks to the writer of #788. I would really love to talk to you someday, though I don’t know how. Thank you again. Thank you for the validation. Thank you for letting me know that I am not alone. May Allah heal you. Whatever you are going through, may Allah help you to overcome that. I know it's not simple or easy, but I really hope one fine morning you wake up and realize everything is okay, and the painful, suffocating feelings in your heart are gone. I don't know how that is gonna happen, but I really wish you get to spend your nights peacefully, and get to fall asleep without a heavy heart.

Obviously, I am not comfortable with sharing my personal details publicly. (And I don't have any intention to gain any kind of sympathy.) Yes I have tried to talk with my friends and family. Every time I did, they were worried about me, which I absolutely hated. Yeah, it's natural as they love me. But at this point, I think I need professional help, as it is difficult for me to continue with my daily activities. I have always handled all of my problems technically. For distraction, nowadays I have started to spend more than half of my day in the gym, even though I am physically fit, just to pass the time. I started spending my nights in prayer, trying to spend more time with family and friends, doing the things I love. But in the end, nothing is helping. It was 9 or 10 traumatic events that have brought me to this point, and each of them has shattered my whole world at various times throughout my life. Every time I thought what did I do to deserve all these. But I trust Allah. Every time I have told myself that everything will be okay. And every single time I managed to stand up again on my own. I am really proud of myself for that. I have been suffering for almost 10 years now. Every now and then, a new devastating event adds to the list. I have tried different antidepressant pills suggested by doctors for a long time and tried opening up to my friends. But now in KUET, everyone of my friends knows me as the most cheerful person around them. The perfect life, the perfect friend circle, the perfect supportive parents anyone can ever have, decent academics, as an always chill, cheerful girl. And now, I don’t have anyone to share the things happening inside me. I tried; obviously, they were supportive, they listened to everything I wanted to share. But none of these are helping.

Anyway, thank you all. Maybe I will try to seek professional help. Maybe I won't!
Thank you, KUET Whispers, for being the platform we can trust. Maybe this is my last post here. If I have annoyed you with my lengthy post, I am extremely sorry for that. Pardon my mistakes kindly, my mind is not in the right place. I really wanted to thank the people who helped me. Stay blessed, everyone!

-Author of #784

text:
#787
I, in a sense, influenced the decision of my close friend to attend KUET, considering her safety and the neutral political situation I had heard about. I had assured her it would be fine. Now, I find myself regretting it all and feeling scared, especially after the recent posts that have come to light regarding the current situation.
It felt surreal to see some friends from private universities hanging out in their cafeterias, gossiping, and spending time in a relaxed manner – the kind of university life everyone, or at least I, used to dream about.
This is a plea to my beloved seniors: Please, let's break the tradition. We respect you, and some fun is okay, but I don't want to be horrified by the choices I've made. Give us the best campus experience possible, and we vow to protect and cherish it.
-Anonymous(Batch'23)

text:
#775

Reply to #774,

Well, I tried to convey the same message,in a bit of different manner a few months ago.The situation was different back then and the post was written focusing more on the situation which we were going through at that time.Back then, I was harshly criticized by someone-who wrote a long paragraph and attacked me by questioning my dedication towards the department and hall I am attached to.Also he said,"You must be ashamed to post this "- and many other stuff what a so called brainwashed guy at KUET used to say before all this revolution started.
Interesting thing was,both my post ,and the comment which attacked me,was getting reach almost at an equal rate which actually gave me some optimism about the fact that- people in here are not actually that গাব,rather they're scared to speak out loud !

Though I agree with the critique on one point. I also don't want my batchmates to work their ass off while I just sleep at my bedroom...I feel bad.Personally speaking, I actually tried to help my batchmates whenever they really needed my assistance.

কিন্তু সেটা আমি ব্যাচমেট বন্ধুদের বাঁচাতে চেয়েছি বলে,নৈতিক দায়িত্ব মনে করে।হুমকির উপর চাপ দিয়ে কামলা খাটতে যারা বাধ্য করে,ওদের আমি কোনোদিন ক্ষমা করবনা।ওদের সাফাই যারা গাবে ওদেরও না !

I don't know if he did,or the admin deleted that comment within 20-30 mins of that comment being posted.

After all these days having passed, I finally see the positive changes at last...We have finally started to criticize the toxic culture and customs that makes Teligati a hell. I must salute those seniors who have exposed a clear image of how and why this weird chain of command at KUET works.The brainwashed people are coming out of their misconceptions once they used to preach.

What has given me tremendous hope today- Even that commentor from that day- has given 'love' on this post. 

That's the change ! That's the revolution ! 
This must continue !

একটি সুন্দর বিষবাষ্পমুক্ত তেলিগতির আশায় -

-Author of #199

text:
#774

"I wonder, even if the majority withdraws themselves from politics and 'politicals' are morally banned, would the RAG culture and forcing culture will gain hate anytime soon? 

There is a popular narrative - ""This is the first year you need to have a taste of everything"", used to force freshers to join many events. Many freshers find these events improper from moral/religious viewpoints.

I urge you - please stop it, If someone's family views / religious views restrain them from joining any events, then doing so one would find shameful. Let them be themselves, whoever they are that's their identity, you have no right to change a person to a form you think is good. If someone's introverted, let one be.

One may argue that one's mature enough at this level to choose. Well, does one get to choose in the first year?
A teenager, leaving home for the first time, in a totally new environment, with no one to trust, and living in messes', becomes very vulnerable at this point. 

To admin - I have tried posting this type of post before, but you seem to have some bias.  I hope you'd also be open to others' views.
- anonymous

You were never a part of my life. You came suddenly and unexpectedly when I was very happy with my own life. After you came, lots of my existence has changed. Maybe I lost myself, maybe I came to know the feelings of love or the heartache of losing someone special. To be very honest, I always thought I'm a selfish person and I love myself so much that I never thought to love someone else that much. But after knowing you, loving you, all my existences are questioning me.... they are saying it's not you, we don't know you, even I also think this is not me. I was so bold but how I became so soft for you. Trust me I'm only soft for you. 
There is a dialogue in series "Breaking Bad" which is "I loved her more than anything". Yes, I love you more than anything. I love you and after seeing you, I realized I'll never love anyone the way I love you. 
- anonymous

I'm feeling bad and unsure why. There are many things weighing on me, making it hard to shake off this feeling. It seems like I once had many friends, but now it feels like I have no one. They all have different groups, and somehow, I feel singled out in each of them. I feel alone. Sometimes, I act a bit childish. It's not because I hold grudges; I'm just sad about their behavior, especially those I care about. I try to forget everything, but every new incident reminds me of the previous one. My friends might still talk to me if I reach out, but it feels like they don't really need me. This makes me feel even more suffocated. On top of that, I'm struggling with my self-worth. I feel like I'm not good at anything and lack confidence in myself.
-anonymous

text:
#726
(Starting with the line from the post #663) 
Apparently I had lost the game, before I even got a chance to play.

An open letter to you,
Hey there. How have you been? How's life? Today, I finally mustered the courage to pour my heart out here. I don't know if you'll see this or realize it's meant for you, but I am gonna say these.
I still remember the day you sent me a friend request on facebook. My facebook was flooded with requests from batchmates and seniors after getting into KUET, but yours felt different. I don’t know why, but I started smiling and blushing just looking at your dp. I even showed it to my sister (she still asks about you, and I have nothing but silence as a response). And then the day you first texted me— I was making coffee, and I paused everything just to reply to you. These details may seem trivial, but they are treasures to me.
Though we exchanged only a few sentences that day, I talked about it with my best friends for hours. "Guess who texted me!!"
My dearest,
I loved everything about you. My heart knows how much I wanted you. My heart knows how much I cherished our conversations, the days we talked about absolutely nothing important. The first time I saw you, and the times our paths crossed. And then, the day I lost you. It’s been months since we last talked, but my heart still aches. I don’t know how you felt about me, but even after so many days, I can't look at another man, let alone think about starting something new with someone else. Whenever you are around, my heart shatters. And the thoughts of what we could be if only....
Okay, then! I will wait to see the lucky girl who will get you. I want you to have all the happiness in the world. I have nothing but good wishes for you.
Let's meet each other in another universe, wander into a vibrant forest together, listen to our favorite music together, talk about our dreams on a stormy night together. This time, let's fall for each other there.
-anonymous

#725
If you love people, show them that you love them. Love deeply. But keep the rest of yourself in order. Don't take your life at the crossroads, keep it right on the road. Even if you are deeply in love with someone, don't convince him that you cannot live without him. You will die. Whenever you expose yourself as the 'weak' in a relationship, you will be trapped. 
Humans like to crush and extract the juice from things easily available at hand.

If the moon was not in the sky or in the hands of people, people would not write poetry about it.
Learn to love people, don't worship them. If you make them gods/goddess to worship, they remain gods and goddesses. They will be worshiped alright, but you won't get the 'Songsar' that you wanted so much

text:
#687
Reply to #683

Hey, fella. Let me share my story.

There was a time when I literally begged for companionship. In my first year, people started forming friendships through introduction sessions, in the halls, with messmates, during adda sessions, playing, and whatnot. I avoided those because I wouldn't have been sane with all those.

Everyone would go out with groups, and I would find out only after the trip. I managed to join once or twice, but I felt left out during those trips. Watching their stories and seeing them enjoy themselves was painful; I felt like I was missing out on everything. So, I actively sought out opportunities to go out, asking people in the labs if they had plans after the lab. Sometimes, I would find out later that they went out and excluded me. Are they obligated to invite me? Absolutely not. It was a request. I totally understand that I was and am not someone interesting to hang out with; I can't make stories and don't talk about myself much because there's nothing interesting about me. So their ignoring me is totally understandable.

Fast forward to now, I am a very active listener. I have always been like that, but now I have found people who are sick of the complex KUETian interpersonal relationships and want a simple person to hang out with. They tell their stories and rants, and I listen. Now, I also go here and there, which I craved from the beginning of my life at KUET. Somehow, Allah now provides me with people whenever I want to go out. Before that, I used to go places on my own.

This story came out to be a long rant. Don't loose hope. People, places, and relationships with friends change. The pictures and videos you are watching on stories are 70% অন্তঃসারশূন্য. If you observe closely, you can find this out. So don't get too sad. 

But শুকনা কথায় যেমন চিড়া ভিজে না, তেমনই আঙ্গুর ফল টক বলে এই ঘোরাঘুরির ব্যাপারে নিজের মনকে ঠান্ডা করা যায় না। I totally understand it because I am/was a sufferer of this. 

Try, but don't try too hard. Don't let yourself down. Always remember to be genuine. Try to help people in their need, and stand by their side when they are at their most vulnerable stage. Don't think about what you would get by helping others; the help may not always, but can, come back to you quadrupled. Let them see how you are and how your heart is. I hope people get to know you and feel lucky to be with you. Also, if you like to contact me, mail me at: hey_is_that_you@proton.me.

N.B. I am an active listener. If you talk, I'm all ears. If not, that's also okay for me; sitting down silently with a cup of tea is also fine for me. I prefer roaming around. 

-Someone from batch '20

text:
#686
I am an odd human sometimes wondering if I am no longer a human. Not in a good way exactly. There's a term to describe people like me, I think it's called "Neuro divergent" but those with a limited vocabulary would like to use the adjectives "weirdo" or "crazy". Or perhaps I am, a potential psychopath. It doesn't matter. Except I think about it a lot when I have mental breakdowns when I cry unstoppably for half an hour straight till I have difficulty breathing and start feeling like I am burning. Everything feels like hell. You must think I am lonely. Otherwise who the fuck writes their sob story anonymously in an university student scribble page. But no, I have one or two friends, who will probably hear it all and send me virtual hugs or would call me up right then. But the truth is I approach them again and again, I think they hate me. But sometimes people hate me for no reason at all. The friendship drifts apart after being close for a year or two. Sometimes the friendship doesn't even form. It's only me rooting for my friendships with certain beings that will never happen. 
I don't have the courage to damage my self worth anymore. It's like I am begging people to be my friend. It's like I am ghostly and unsuitable to be a friend. Is it the face or the posture, attitude or the lameness? What is in me that hurts you, that detaches me from you and makes me lonely?

 I had a near death situation in my life. When I was 12-13, some girls at my class who were 2-3 years older than me took me to the fourth floor to bully me. The bullying got really intense at one point that I was nearly cornered to a wall, my hands being held by a girl and they were giving me death threats. My so called "best friend" at school, after finishing her Exams(I submitted my copy earlier that day) was walking around the school with another girl. She saw me in that situation (by accident I must say), she just had an eye contact with me and left me alone. I did not even tell this incident to my Mum after coming home. I wanted to die. I felt pathetic. Not only I got bullied to death but also I got betrayed. Yet I made myself calm down saying perhaps my friend couldn't do much that time even if she tried to help. I dedicated all my time to academics then. The bullying stopped. I became a teacher's pet. Life was going well, only I had extreme trust issues, depression, loneliness and suicidal issues. There are 4 little scars in my hand always reminding me of my pathetic suicidal attempts. However, I decided to open up to people that I really liked to befriend for the last two years. They didn't like me back though. Interacting with these humans reminded me of my npc bullied days when I got hurt by people, apologized to them and thought how lucky I was to be stepped on by those glory feet. Recently, I have gotten lonelier, again, chronically and acutely. If I learn to live my life alone, will it make me more socially acceptable? One of the people I met here, vibed a lot, said to me once "people don't know what are the boundaries you have set around yourself .That's why things get weird." I know I am the black sheep here. I apologize heartily. Another of my classmates said "I always thought you wanted to be alone and never craved friends. That's why I intentionally ignored you." I would let you people be you. I didn't want to write these stuffs here. Cuz it feels lame trust me, putting your heart out there after conducting an autopsy on yourself, in front of strangers. Dear people, whom I had called friends here, you guys had every right to save me, every opportunity to stop all these mishaps that are happening right now yet you looked the other way when I was falling apart. 

"I to whom there is not beauty enough in moon or tree; to whom the touch of one person with another is all, yet who cannot grasp even that, who am so imperfect, so weak, so unspeakably lonely. There I sat."-
Virginia Woolf, The Waves

-anonymous
